PPE is donned before entry and doffed dirtiest-first (gloves, eye protection, gown) with hand hygiene as the final step, all to prevent transmission to other clients.
correct.zh: 艰难梭菌是一种形成孢子、经粪-口途径传播的病原体,需要采取接触隔离、用肥皂流动水洗手(酒精不能杀灭孢子)、使用专用器械,以及使用含氯(杀孢子)消毒剂清洁表面。进入前穿戴PPE,脱卸时遵循最脏先脱原则(手套、护目镜、隔离衣),最后完成手卫生,以防止传播给其他患者。

testTakingStrategy.en: Two C. difficile flags override defaults: soap-and-water (not alcohol) and bleach (not quat) surfaces. Otherwise apply standard contact-precaution and PPE-doffing rules.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 两个艰难梭菌要点优先于常规做法:用肥皂流动水(而非酒精)、用含氯漂白剂(而非季铵盐)消毒表面。其余遵循标准的接触隔离和PPE脱卸规则。

glossary.defZh: 常在抗生素治疗后过度增殖、引起腹泻和结肠炎的产芽孢厌氧菌
病原体经被粪便污染的手、物品或食物进入口腔而传播

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items about preventive screening and immunization at a wellness visit.
stem.zh: 查阅患者病历,回答关于健康体检中预防性筛查与免疫接种的案例分析题。

title.en: Unfolding case: adult wellness and prevention visit
title.zh: 情境演变案例:成人健康与预防保健就诊

summary.en: A 66-year-old client with well-controlled type 2 diabetes presents to a primary care clinic for a routine wellness visit. The nurse reviews preventive care and immunization status.
summary.zh: 一名66岁、2型糖尿病控制良好的患者到初级保健诊所进行常规健康体检。护士审查其预防保健和免疫接种情况。

content.en: Type 2 diabetes, on metformin. No known immunodeficiency. Immunizations: last Td/Tdap 12 years ago; no influenza vaccine this season; no pneumococcal vaccine; no zoster (shingles) vaccine. Never screened for colorectal cancer. Non-smoker.
content.zh: 2型糖尿病,服用二甲双胍。无已知免疫缺陷。免疫接种情况:上次Td/Tdap为12年前;本季未接种流感疫苗;未接种肺炎球菌疫苗;未接种带状疱疹疫苗。从未进行结直肠癌筛查。不吸烟。

stem.en: For each immunization, indicate whether it is currently recommended (indicated now) for this 66-year-old client or not indicated at this visit.
stem.zh: 对于每种疫苗,指出对这位66岁患者目前是否推荐接种(当前有指征),还是本次就诊不需要接种。

rows.en: Seasonal influenza vaccine
rows.zh: 季节性流感疫苗

rows.en: Pneumococcal vaccine
rows.zh: 肺炎球菌疫苗

rows.en: Recombinant zoster (shingles) vaccine
rows.zh: 重组带状疱疹疫苗

rows.en: Td/Tdap booster
rows.zh: Td/Tdap加强针

correct.en: All four are indicated. Influenza vaccine is recommended annually for nearly all adults and he has not had it this season. Pneumococcal vaccination is recommended for adults age 50 years or older who have not received a pneumococcal conjugate vaccine or whose history is unknown. Recombinant zoster vaccine is recommended for adults 50 and older. A tetanus-containing booster is due because his last dose was 12 years ago (routine interval is every 10 years).
correct.zh: 四项均有指征。流感疫苗几乎所有成年人每年都应接种,而他本季尚未接种。对于年满50岁且未接种过肺炎球菌结合疫苗或接种史不明的成年人,建议接种肺炎球菌疫苗。重组带状疱疹疫苗建议50岁及以上成年人接种。含破伤风成分的加强针也已到期,因为上次接种距今12年(常规间隔为每10年一次)。

byChoice.en: Annual influenza vaccine is recommended for essentially all adults; he is unvaccinated this season.
byChoice.zh: 几乎所有成人均推荐每年接种流感疫苗,而他本季未接种。

byChoice.en: Pneumococcal vaccination is indicated because the client is older than the current adult age threshold and has no prior pneumococcal vaccination documented.
byChoice.zh: 该患者年龄已超过当前成年人接种肺炎球菌疫苗的年龄阈值,且无既往肺炎球菌疫苗接种记录,因此有接种指征。

byChoice.en: Recombinant zoster vaccine is recommended for adults 50 and older to prevent shingles and its complications.
byChoice.zh: 重组带状疱疹疫苗推荐50岁及以上成人接种,以预防带状疱疹及其并发症。

byChoice.en: A tetanus-diphtheria booster is due every 10 years; his last was 12 years ago.
byChoice.zh: 破伤风-白喉加强针每10年一次,而他上次接种在12年前,已到期。

testTakingStrategy.en: Anchor adult vaccines to age/interval triggers: flu annually, pneumococcal at 50 (CDC 2024 update), zoster at 50, Td/Tdap every 10 years.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 把成人疫苗与年龄/间隔触发点对应记忆:流感每年、肺炎球菌50岁(CDC 2024 更新)、带状疱疹50岁、Td/Tdap每10年。

glossary.defZh: 非活疫苗,用于预防带状疱疹,推荐≥50岁成人接种
含破伤风、白喉和无细胞百日咳成分的疫苗

stem.en: The client has never been screened for colorectal cancer and has average risk. Which screening should the nurse expect to be recommended?
stem.zh: 该患者从未进行过结直肠癌筛查,且为平均风险人群。护士应预期推荐何种筛查?

options.en: Abdominal ultrasound
options.zh: 腹部超声检查

options.en: No colorectal screening is needed until age 75 regardless of prior screening
options.zh: 无论既往是否筛查,75岁前都无需结直肠筛查

options.en: Colonoscopy (or another approved colorectal screening test)
options.zh: 结肠镜检查(或其他获批的结直肠筛查方法)

options.en: Serum carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) as a screening test
options.zh: 以血清癌胚抗原(CEA)作为筛查方法

correct.en: Average-risk adults should be screened for colorectal cancer beginning at age 45 and continuing through 75; an unscreened 66-year-old is overdue. Colonoscopy is a recommended option (stool-based tests are alternatives). Ultrasound and CEA are not colorectal cancer screening tests.
correct.zh: 平均风险成人应从45岁开始进行结直肠癌筛查,并持续至75岁;一位66岁、从未筛查者已严重滞后。结肠镜是推荐方案之一(粪便检测为替代方案)。腹部超声和CEA均非结直肠癌筛查手段。

byChoice.en: Colonoscopy is a standard recommended colorectal screening for an overdue average-risk adult.
byChoice.zh: 对于筛查滞后的平均风险成人,结肠镜是标准推荐的结直肠筛查方法。

byChoice.en: Screening is recommended from 45 to 75; a never-screened 66-year-old needs it now, not deferral.
byChoice.zh: 筛查推荐于45至75岁进行;一位从未筛查的66岁者现在就需要,而非推迟。

byChoice.en: Abdominal ultrasound is not used to screen for colorectal cancer.
byChoice.zh: 腹部超声不用于结直肠癌筛查。

byChoice.en: CEA is a tumor marker for monitoring known disease, not a screening test.
byChoice.zh: CEA是用于监测已知疾病的肿瘤标志物,而非筛查手段。

testTakingStrategy.en: Know the average-risk colorectal window: start at 45, continue to 75. Tumor markers and ultrasound are not screening tools.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 记住平均风险结直肠筛查窗口:45岁开始、持续至75岁。肿瘤标志物和超声不是筛查工具。

glossary.defZh: 用内镜直接观察结肠并可切除息肉的检查
无个人/家族史等额外危险因素的一般人群风险水平

stem.en: While at the clinic, the client mentions his adult daughter is pregnant and was just diagnosed with leukemia and is receiving chemotherapy. He asks which vaccine she should NOT receive right now. Which response is correct?
stem.zh: 就诊时,患者提到他成年的女儿正在怀孕,且刚被诊断为白血病、正在接受化疗。他询问女儿目前不应接种哪种疫苗。哪项回答正确?

options.en: Live attenuated vaccines (such as MMR or varicella) should be avoided
options.zh: 应避免接种减毒活疫苗(如麻腮风疫苗或水痘疫苗)

options.en: The inactivated influenza vaccine should be avoided
options.zh: 应避免接种灭活流感疫苗

options.en: The tetanus (Tdap) vaccine should be avoided
options.zh: 应避免接种破伤风(Tdap)疫苗

options.en: All vaccines are safe and none should be avoided
options.zh: 所有疫苗都安全,不需要避免任何疫苗

correct.en: Live attenuated vaccines (e.g., MMR, varicella, live nasal influenza) are contraindicated in pregnancy and in significantly immunocompromised clients such as those receiving chemotherapy, because the weakened organism can cause disease. Inactivated vaccines like the injectable influenza and Tdap are not only safe but recommended in pregnancy.
correct.zh: 减毒活疫苗(如麻腮风疫苗、水痘疫苗、鼻喷活流感疫苗)在妊娠期及如接受化疗等明显免疫低下患者中属禁忌,因为减毒的病原体可能致病。而灭活疫苗如注射型流感疫苗和Tdap不仅安全,且在妊娠期推荐接种。

byChoice.en: Live vaccines can replicate and cause infection in pregnant or immunocompromised clients, so they are contraindicated.
byChoice.zh: 活疫苗在妊娠或免疫低下患者体内可复制并致病,故属禁忌。

byChoice.en: Inactivated influenza vaccine is safe and recommended in pregnancy; it should not be avoided.
byChoice.zh: 灭活流感疫苗在妊娠期安全且推荐,不应避免。

byChoice.en: It is not true that all vaccines are safe here; live vaccines are specifically contraindicated.
byChoice.zh: "所有疫苗都安全"并不正确;活疫苗在此情况下明确属禁忌。

byChoice.en: Tdap is an inactivated vaccine recommended during each pregnancy; avoiding it is incorrect.
byChoice.zh: Tdap为灭活疫苗,推荐每次妊娠期接种;避免接种是错误的。

testTakingStrategy.en: Red flag pairs: pregnancy and immunosuppression both contraindicate LIVE vaccines. Inactivated vaccines stay safe.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 危险信号组合:妊娠和免疫抑制都禁忌"活"疫苗。灭活疫苗仍然安全。

glossary.defZh: 含减毒活病原体的疫苗,在免疫低下或妊娠者中禁用
因疾病或治疗(如化疗)导致免疫防御能力下降

stem.en: Which statements by the client indicate correct understanding of his preventive care teaching? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 患者的哪些陈述表明他正确理解了预防保健的健康教育?选择所有适用项。

options.en: "Keeping my blood sugar controlled lowers my risk of complications."
options.zh: "控制好血糖能降低我并发症的风险。"

options.en: "Since I feel healthy, I can skip the colorectal cancer screening."
options.zh: "既然我感觉健康,就可以不做结直肠癌筛查。"

options.en: "I should get a flu shot every year."
options.zh: "我每年都应该打流感疫苗。"

options.en: "The shingles vaccine can help prevent a painful rash and nerve pain."
options.zh: "带状疱疹疫苗有助于预防疼痛性皮疹和神经痛。"

options.en: "Because my last tetanus shot was over 10 years ago, I'm due for a booster."
options.zh: "因为我上次破伤风疫苗已超过10年,我该打加强针了。"

correct.en: Annual influenza vaccination, a tetanus booster every 10 years, glycemic control to reduce diabetic complications, and zoster vaccination to prevent shingles and postherpetic neuralgia all reflect correct understanding. Believing that feeling well removes the need for colorectal screening is a misconception, since colorectal cancer is often asymptomatic early.
correct.zh: 每年接种流感疫苗、每10年一次破伤风加强针、控制血糖以减少糖尿病并发症,以及接种带状疱疹疫苗以预防带状疱疹和疱疹后神经痛,均体现正确理解。认为"感觉健康就无需结直肠筛查"是错误观念,因为结直肠癌早期常无症状。

byChoice.en: Annual flu vaccination is correct and reflects understanding.
byChoice.zh: 每年接种流感疫苗正确,体现了正确理解。

byChoice.en: A 10-year tetanus booster interval is accurate; his is overdue.
byChoice.zh: 破伤风加强针间隔10年准确;他已到期。

byChoice.en: Feeling well does not rule out early, asymptomatic colorectal cancer; this statement is a misconception needing correction.
byChoice.zh: 感觉健康并不能排除早期无症状的结直肠癌;此陈述为误解,需纠正。

byChoice.en: Glycemic control reducing complications is accurate health-promotion understanding.
byChoice.zh: 控制血糖以减少并发症是准确的健康促进认知。

byChoice.en: Zoster vaccine prevents shingles and postherpetic neuralgia; this is correct.
byChoice.zh: 带状疱疹疫苗可预防带状疱疹及疱疹后神经痛;陈述正确。

testTakingStrategy.en: On "correct understanding" items, the wrong choice is usually the one that uses "feeling fine" to skip evidence-based screening.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在"正确理解"类题目中,错误选项通常是以"感觉良好"为由跳过循证筛查的那一项。

glossary.defZh: 带状疱疹消退后持续存在的神经性疼痛
通过早期筛查发现并干预无症状疾病

correct.en: Health promotion for an older adult integrates age- and interval-based immunization (annual influenza, pneumococcal at 65, recombinant zoster at 50, Td/Tdap every 10 years), evidence-based screening (colorectal cancer from 45 to 75 regardless of how well the client feels), and recognition that live vaccines are contraindicated in pregnancy and immunosuppression. Glycemic control further reduces this diabetic client's long-term risk.
correct.zh: 针对老年人的健康促进需综合考虑:基于年龄和间隔的免疫接种(流感每年、肺炎球菌65岁、重组带状疱疹50岁、Td/Tdap每10年);循证筛查(结直肠癌45至75岁,无论自觉是否健康);以及认识到活疫苗在妊娠和免疫抑制者中禁用。控制血糖可进一步降低这位糖尿病患者的长期风险。

testTakingStrategy.en: Tie prevention to triggers: vaccines to age/interval, screening to age window, and contraindications to host status (pregnancy/immunosuppression = no live vaccines).
testTakingStrategy.zh: 将预防与触发点对应:疫苗对应年龄/间隔,筛查对应年龄窗口,禁忌对应宿主状态(妊娠/免疫抑制=不接种活疫苗)。

glossary.defZh: 在疾病发生前进行的干预,如免疫接种

glossary.termZh: 免疫接种实践咨询委员会
glossary.defZh: 制定美国疫苗接种推荐意见的专家委员会

questions.topic: Intimate Partner Violence Screening and Support

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items about screening for and responding to suspected intimate partner violence.
stem.zh: 查阅患者病历,回答关于疑似亲密伴侣暴力的筛查与应对的案例分析题。

title.en: Unfolding case: suspected intimate partner violence in the emergency department
title.zh: 情境演变案例:急诊科疑似亲密伴侣暴力

summary.en: A 29-year-old woman presents to the emergency department with a wrist injury. Her partner stays at the bedside and answers most questions for her. The nurse notes several findings that raise concern.
summary.zh: 一名29岁女性因手腕受伤到急诊科就诊。她的伴侣一直守在床边,并代她回答大部分问题。护士注意到若干引起担忧的发现。

content.en: Chief complaint: 'I fell.' Injury: forearm bruising in various stages of healing plus a new wrist injury. The client avoids eye contact, is anxious, and defers to her partner. The partner is reluctant to leave the room. Delay of two days before seeking care.
content.zh: 主诉:"我摔倒了。"损伤:前臂可见不同愈合阶段的瘀斑,并有新的手腕损伤。患者回避眼神交流,表现焦虑,凡事顺从伴侣。伴侣不愿离开病房。受伤后延迟两天才就医。

stem.en: Which findings should the nurse recognize as cues that raise concern for intimate partner violence? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 护士应将哪些发现识别为提示亲密伴侣暴力的可疑线索?选择所有适用项。

options.en: The client requesting a written list of her discharge medications
options.zh: 患者要求一份书面的出院用药清单

options.en: Injuries in various stages of healing
options.zh: 处于不同愈合阶段的损伤

options.en: A partner who answers for the client and is reluctant to leave
options.zh: 伴侣代患者回答且不愿离开

options.en: An explanation of injury that is inconsistent with the findings
options.zh: 受伤解释与体征不相符

options.en: A delay of two days before seeking care
options.zh: 受伤后延迟两天才就医

correct.en: Injuries at different healing stages, delayed care, an injury story that does not match the physical findings, and a controlling partner who answers for the client and will not leave are all classic red flags for intimate partner violence. Asking for a medication list is normal, appropriate engagement in care.
correct.zh: 处于不同愈合阶段的损伤、延迟就医、受伤经过与体征不符,以及一个代答且不愿离开的控制型伴侣,都是亲密伴侣暴力的典型危险信号。索要用药清单则是正常、恰当地参与自身护理的表现。

byChoice.en: Multiple injuries at different ages suggest repeated trauma over time.
byChoice.zh: 多处不同时期的损伤提示反复发生的创伤。

byChoice.en: Delayed care-seeking can indicate restricted access or fear of a partner.
byChoice.zh: 延迟就医可能提示就医受限或惧怕伴侣。

byChoice.en: An injury explanation inconsistent with the findings is a hallmark of abuse.
byChoice.zh: 受伤解释与体征不符是虐待的典型标志。

byChoice.en: A partner controlling the interaction and refusing to leave is a controlling behavior associated with IPV.
byChoice.zh: 伴侣掌控交流且拒绝离开是与亲密伴侣暴力相关的控制行为。

byChoice.en: Requesting a medication list is appropriate self-care engagement, not a warning sign.
byChoice.zh: 索要用药清单是恰当的自我护理参与,而非警示信号。

testTakingStrategy.en: IPV cues cluster around inconsistency, delay, repeated injury, and a controlling companion. A normal care request is the outlier.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 亲密伴侣暴力的线索集中在:经过不符、就医延迟、反复受伤和有控制欲的陪同者。正常的护理请求是例外项。

glossary.defZh: 现任或前任伴侣施加的身体、性、心理或经济虐待

stem.en: What is the most appropriate nursing action to screen the client for intimate partner violence?
stem.zh: 为筛查患者是否遭受亲密伴侣暴力,最恰当的护理措施是什么?

options.en: Avoid raising the topic unless the client brings it up first
options.zh: 除非患者主动提起,否则避免谈及该话题

options.en: Find a private moment to interview the client alone, away from the partner
options.zh: 找一个私密时机,在伴侣不在场时单独询问患者

options.en: Ask the client about abuse directly in front of the partner
options.zh: 当着伴侣的面直接询问患者是否遭受虐待

options.en: Confront the partner about the suspicious injuries
options.zh: 就可疑损伤当面质问伴侣

correct.en: Screening for IPV must occur privately, with the client alone, so she can answer honestly without fear of the partner. The nurse can use a routine reason (such as a test or specimen) to ask the partner to step out. Asking in front of the partner endangers the client, confronting the partner can escalate violence, and waiting silently misses the chance to offer help.
correct.zh: 亲密伴侣暴力的筛查必须在私密环境下、患者单独在场时进行,使其能在不惧怕伴侣的情况下如实回答。护士可借常规理由(如做检查或留取标本)请伴侣暂时离开。当着伴侣的面询问会危及患者,质问伴侣可能激化暴力,而沉默等待则会错失提供帮助的机会。

byChoice.en: Interviewing the client alone creates the safety and privacy needed for an honest disclosure.
byChoice.zh: 单独询问患者可营造如实倾诉所需的安全与私密环境。

byChoice.en: Asking in front of the partner puts the client at risk and discourages honest answers.
byChoice.zh: 当着伴侣的面询问会使患者面临风险,也使其难以如实回答。

byChoice.en: Confronting the suspected abuser can provoke retaliation and escalate danger to the client.
byChoice.zh: 质问疑似施暴者可能招致报复,加重患者的危险。

byChoice.en: Staying silent forfeits an important opportunity to screen and offer resources.
byChoice.zh: 保持沉默会放弃筛查并提供资源的重要机会。

testTakingStrategy.en: The first rule of IPV screening: get the client alone. Never screen or confront in front of the suspected abuser.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 亲密伴侣暴力筛查的第一原则:让患者单独在场。绝不在疑似施暴者面前进行筛查或质问。

glossary.defZh: 对尚未明确的问题进行系统询问或评估以早期识别

stem.en: Once alone, the client tearfully discloses that her partner hit her but says, 'I'm not ready to leave him.' What is the nurse's priority action?
stem.zh: 在单独相处后,患者含泪坦言伴侣打了她,但说"我还没准备好离开他"。护士的首要措施是什么?

options.en: Insist that she leave the partner immediately before discharge
options.zh: 坚持要求她在出院前立即离开伴侣

options.en: Tell her she must press charges against her partner today
options.zh: 告诉她今天必须对伴侣提起诉讼

options.en: Assess her immediate safety and help develop a safety plan, including resources she can use
options.zh: 评估她当前的安全状况,并协助制定安全计划,包括可使用的求助资源

options.en: Reassure her that the situation will likely improve on its own
options.zh: 安慰她说情况很可能会自行好转

correct.en: The nurse respects the client's autonomy while prioritizing safety: assess immediate danger and collaboratively develop a safety plan and provide resources (hotline, shelter, escape plan). Pressuring her to leave or press charges removes her control, can escalate danger, and may drive her away from help. Falsely reassuring her is unsafe.
correct.zh: 护士应在尊重患者自主权的同时优先保障安全:评估当前危险程度,与其共同制定安全计划并提供资源(求助热线、庇护所、撤离计划)。强迫她离开或起诉会剥夺其控制权、可能加重危险,并可能使她拒绝接受帮助。虚假地安慰她则不安全。

byChoice.en: Safety assessment plus a collaborative safety plan supports the client without removing her autonomy.
byChoice.zh: 安全评估加上共同制定安全计划,既保护患者又不剥夺其自主权。

byChoice.en: Insisting she leave overrides her readiness and can escalate the abuser's violence.
byChoice.zh: 强迫她离开违背其意愿,且可能激化施暴者的暴力行为。

byChoice.en: Demanding she press charges removes her control; legal action is her decision.
byChoice.zh: 强令她起诉会剥夺其控制权;是否采取法律行动应由她自己决定。

byChoice.en: Reassuring her it will improve on its own is false and unsafe; IPV tends to escalate.
byChoice.zh: 安慰她说会自行好转既不真实也不安全;亲密伴侣暴力往往会逐步升级。

testTakingStrategy.en: Support autonomy + safety. The right answer assesses danger and plans for safety; wrong answers command, judge, or falsely reassure.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 兼顾自主与安全。正确答案评估危险并制定安全计划;错误答案则是命令、评判或虚假安慰。

glossary.defZh: 为暴力受害者预先制定的、在危险时迅速获得安全的具体方案
患者自行做出与自身相关决定的权利

stem.en: For each nurse statement or action, indicate whether it is therapeutic/appropriate or non-therapeutic/inappropriate when supporting this client.
stem.zh: 对于护士的每项陈述或行为,指出在支持该患者时是治疗性/恰当的,还是非治疗性/不恰当的。

rows.en: "This is not your fault, and you did not deserve to be hurt."
rows.zh: "这不是你的错,你不该受到伤害。"

rows.en: "I'm concerned for your safety. Here is a hotline number you can keep."
rows.zh: "我很担心你的安全。这是一个你可以保留的求助热线号码。"

rows.en: "Why do you stay with someone who hurts you?"
rows.zh: "你为什么还和一个伤害你的人待在一起?"

rows.en: Document the client's own words and objective findings accurately in the record
rows.zh: 在病历中准确记录患者的原话和客观体征

correct.en: Affirming that the abuse is not the client's fault, expressing concern while providing resources, and accurately documenting the client's own words and objective findings are all therapeutic and support safety. Asking 'why do you stay' is judgmental, implies blame, and is non-therapeutic.
correct.zh: 明确告知患者虐待不是她的错、在表达关心的同时提供资源,以及准确记录患者原话和客观体征,都是治疗性的并有助于保障安全。质问"你为什么还留下来"带有评判、暗示责备,属非治疗性。

byChoice.en: Reassuring the client she is not to blame counters the self-blame common in IPV and is therapeutic.
byChoice.zh: 向患者明确她无过错,可化解亲密伴侣暴力中常见的自责,属治疗性。

byChoice.en: Expressing concern and discreetly offering a hotline supports safety and connects her to resources.
byChoice.zh: 表达关心并谨慎提供求助热线,可保障安全并将其与资源连接。

byChoice.en: 'Why do you stay' is judgmental and blaming, which shuts down trust and is non-therapeutic.
byChoice.zh: "你为什么还留下来"带有评判与责备,会破坏信任,属非治疗性。

byChoice.en: Accurate documentation of the client's words and objective findings is essential and appropriate.
byChoice.zh: 准确记录患者原话和客观体征至关重要且恰当。

testTakingStrategy.en: Therapeutic = nonjudgmental, validating, resource-offering. 'Why' questions that imply blame are almost always non-therapeutic.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 治疗性=不评判、给予肯定、提供资源。带有责备意味的"为什么"提问几乎总是非治疗性。

glossary.defZh: 以患者为中心、建立信任并促进表达的沟通方式
客观、准确地记录评估发现和患者陈述

correct.en: Responding to suspected intimate partner violence requires recognizing cues (inconsistent injury history, delayed care, repeated injuries, controlling partner), screening the client privately and alone, then prioritizing safety through a collaborative safety plan and resources while honoring the client's autonomy. Communication must be nonjudgmental and validating, and documentation must be accurate and objective.
correct.zh: 应对疑似亲密伴侣暴力需要:识别线索(受伤经过不符、延迟就医、反复受伤、控制型伴侣);在私密、单独的环境中筛查患者;随后在尊重患者自主权的前提下,通过共同制定安全计划和提供资源来优先保障安全。沟通必须不带评判、给予肯定,记录必须准确、客观。

testTakingStrategy.en: Across IPV items: interview alone, never confront the abuser, support (don't command) autonomy, stay nonjudgmental, and document objectively.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 贯穿亲密伴侣暴力题目的原则:单独询问、绝不质问施暴者、支持(而非命令)患者自主、保持不评判、客观记录。

glossary.defZh: 亲密伴侣暴力常呈现紧张积累、暴力发作与缓和示好交替的模式
法律要求报告特定虐待(如儿童、老人);对有行为能力成人的伴侣暴力报告要求因辖区而异

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items.
stem.zh: 查阅患者病历并回答案例分析问题。

title.zh: 进展性案例：疑似甲状腺危象

summary.en: A 34-year-old female client is brought to the emergency department by her spouse. The spouse reports the client has been increasingly restless, confused, and sweating profusely over the last 12 hours. The client has a history of Graves' disease but stopped taking methimazole three weeks ago due to gastrointestinal upset.
summary.zh: 一名34岁女性患者由配偶陪同来到急诊室。配偶诉患者在过去12小时内越来越烦躁、意识模糊并大量出汗。患者有毒性弥漫性甲状腺肿（Graves病）病史，但因胃肠道不适于三周前停用甲巯咪唑。

content.en:
Vital Signs:
Temperature: 104.2 °F (40.1 °C)
Heart Rate: 152 bpm (irregular)
Blood Pressure: 168/94 mm Hg
Respiratory Rate: 28 breaths/min
SpO2: 95% on room air

Nursing Assessment:
Neurological: Extremely agitated, tremors present in bilateral upper extremities, oriented to person only.
Cardiovascular: Tachycardic, palpable bounding pulses.
Skin: Flushed, hot, and profusely diaphoretic.
Neck: Diffuse anterior neck enlargement (goiter) noted.
content.zh:
生命体征：
体温：104.2 °F (40.1 °C)
心率：152 次/分 (不规则)
血压：168/94 mm Hg
呼吸频率：28 次/分
SpO2：室内空气下95%

护理评估：
神经系统：极度烦躁，双上肢可见震颤，仅对人有定向力。
心血管系统：心动过速，可触及洪脉。
皮肤：潮红、发烫、大量出汗。
颈部：可见颈前弥漫性肿大（甲状腺肿）。

content.en:
Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH): < 0.01 mIU/L (Normal: 0.4 - 4.0 mIU/L)
Free Thyroxine (Free T4): 5.8 ng/dL (Normal: 0.8 - 2.8 ng/dL)
ECG: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response (RVR)
content.zh:
促甲状腺激素 (TSH)：< 0.01 mIU/L (正常值：0.4 - 4.0 mIU/L)
游离甲状腺素 (Free T4)：5.8 ng/dL (正常值：0.8 - 2.8 ng/dL)
心电图：心房颤动伴快速心室率 (RVR)

content.en:
1. 0.9% Sodium Chloride IV at 150 mL/hr
2. Propranolol 1 mg IV push slowly every 5 minutes until heart rate < 100 bpm
3. Propylthiouracil (PTU) 400 mg PO (or via NG tube) STAT
4. Lugol's iodine solution 5 drops PO every 6 hours
5. Hydrocortisone 100 mg IV STAT
content.zh:
1. 0.9%氯化钠静脉滴注，150 mL/hr
2. 普萘洛尔 1 mg 缓慢静脉推注，每5分钟一次，直至心率 < 100 次/分
3. 丙硫氧嘧啶 (PTU) 400 mg 立即口服 (或经胃管注入)
4. 复方碘溶液 (Lugol液) 5 滴 口服，每6小时一次
5. 氢化可的松 100 mg 立即静脉注射

content.en: The client has received IV fluids, propranolol, PTU, Lugol's iodine, and hydrocortisone as prescribed. The client's agitation has decreased, and continuous telemetry demonstrates normal sinus rhythm. A complete blood count (CBC) with differential and a comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) were drawn.
content.zh: 患者已按医嘱接受静脉补液、普萘洛尔、PTU、Lugol液和氢化可的松治疗。患者烦躁情绪有所缓解，持续心电监护显示正常窦性心律。已抽取全血细胞计数 (CBC) 伴分类及综合代谢指标 (CMP) 样本。

stem.en: The nurse analyzes the client's initial 0800 triage presentation. For each finding, indicate if the cue is expected in uncomplicated hyperthyroidism, or if it specifically indicates a transition to life-threatening thyroid storm.
stem.zh: 护士分析患者0800最初的分诊表现。针对每一项体征，指出该线索是单纯性甲状腺功能亢进的预期表现，还是明确提示已转变为危及生命的甲状腺危象。

rows.en: Temperature of 104.2 °F (40.1 °C)
Diffuse anterior neck enlargement
rows.zh: 颈前弥漫性肿大

rows.en: Severe agitation and confusion
rows.zh: 极度烦躁与意识模糊

rows.en: Heart rate of 152 bpm
rows.zh: 心率 152 次/分

correct.en: Thyroid storm is an extreme, life-threatening exacerbation of hyperthyroidism. While hyperthyroidism causes heat intolerance, extreme hyperpyrexia (e.g., 104.2 °F) is a hallmark of thyroid storm. Mild anxiety is common in hyperthyroidism, but severe agitation, delirium, and confusion indicate CNS dysfunction specific to thyroid storm. Resting tachycardia (90-110 bpm) is expected in hyperthyroidism, but extreme tachycardia (>130 bpm) or arrhythmias like atrial fibrillation signify thyroid storm. A goiter is an anatomical feature of Graves' disease and hyperthyroidism, not a specific acute marker of a storm.
correct.zh: 甲状腺危象是甲状腺功能亢进的一种极端、危及生命的恶化形式。虽然甲亢会导致怕热，但极端高热（如 104.2 °F）是甲状腺危象的标志。轻度焦虑在甲亢中很常见，但极度烦躁、谵妄和意识模糊表明中枢神经系统功能障碍，这是甲状腺危象的特有表现。静息心动过速（90-110 次/分）是甲亢的预期表现，但极度心动过速（>130 次/分）或心律失常（如心房颤动）则标志着甲状腺危象。甲状腺肿是Graves病和甲亢的解剖学特征，并非危象的特异性急性标志。

byChoice.en: Extreme hyperpyrexia is a defining criterion for thyroid storm due to a massive surge in metabolic rate.
byChoice.zh: 极端高热是甲状腺危象的诊断标准之一，原因是代谢率出现了大规模的激增。

byChoice.en: A goiter reflects chronic glandular enlargement in Graves' disease, seen in uncomplicated hyperthyroidism.
byChoice.zh: 甲状腺肿反映了Graves病中慢性的腺体增大，可见于单纯性甲亢。

byChoice.en: CNS dysfunction, ranging from extreme agitation to coma, differentiates thyroid storm from standard hyperthyroidism.
byChoice.zh: 从中枢神经系统功能障碍（从极度烦躁到昏迷）是区分甲状腺危象与标准甲亢的重要标志。

byChoice.en: Severe tachyarrhythmias (often >130-140 bpm) with cardiovascular compromise indicate progression to thyroid storm.
byChoice.zh: 伴有心血管功能受损的严重快速型心律失常（通常>130-140次/分）提示病情进展为甲状腺危象。

testTakingStrategy.en: Distinguish between chronic physiological adaptations to high thyroid levels and the acute, decompensated signs of metabolic crisis. High fever, CNS changes, and extreme tachycardia are the triad of thyroid storm.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 区分对高甲状腺素水平的慢性生理适应与代谢危机的急性失代偿体征。高热、中枢神经系统改变和极度心动过速是甲状腺危象的三联征。

glossary.defZh: 一种罕见但危及生命的严重甲亢并发症，特征为高热、严重心动过速和意识改变。
体温异常升高，通常指体温达到或超过40.0°C (104.0°F) 或以上。

stem.en: Based on the 0830 orders, the nurse prepares to administer the prescribed medications. Drag and drop the medications into the correct order of administration to safely manage the thyroid storm.
stem.zh: 根据0830的医嘱，护士准备给予处方药物。请拖拽药物并按正确的给药顺序排列，以安全地处理甲状腺危象。

options.en: Administer IV propranolol
options.zh: 静脉注射普萘洛尔

options.en: Administer IV hydrocortisone
options.zh: 静脉注射氢化可的松

options.en: Administer Lugol's iodine solution
options.zh: 给予复方碘溶液 (Lugol液)

options.en: Administer propylthiouracil (PTU)
options.zh: 给予丙硫氧嘧啶 (PTU)

correct.en: The pharmacological sequence in thyroid storm is critical to prevent exacerbating the condition. First, a beta-blocker (propranolol) is administered immediately to block peripheral sympathetic effects, lowering the dangerously high heart rate and preventing cardiovascular collapse. Second, an antithyroid medication like PTU is given to block the synthesis of new thyroid hormones. Third, an iodine solution (Lugol's) is given to block the release of pre-formed thyroid hormones. Crucially, iodine must be delayed for 1-2 hours AFTER PTU; if given first, iodine will act as substrate and fuel massive new thyroid hormone synthesis. Fourth, corticosteroids (hydrocortisone) are administered to treat relative adrenal insufficiency caused by hypermetabolism and to block the peripheral conversion of T4 to active T3.
correct.zh: 甲状腺危象的药理学用药顺序对于防止病情恶化至关重要。第一，必须立即给予β受体阻滞剂（普萘洛尔）以阻断外周交感神经效应，降低危险的高心率并预防心血管崩溃。第二，给予抗甲状腺药物如PTU以阻断新甲状腺激素的合成。第三，给予碘溶液（Lugol液）以阻断已生成的甲状腺激素的释放。关键在于，碘剂必须在PTU给药后1-2小时延迟使用；如果先给碘，碘将作为底物加速大量新甲状腺激素的合成。第四，给予皮质类固醇（氢化可的松）以治疗由高代谢引起的相对肾上腺功能不全，并阻断外周T4向活性T3的转化。

byChoice.en: PTU must be given before iodine to inhibit the thyroid gland's ability to use the upcoming iodine to synthesize more hormones.
byChoice.zh: 必须在碘剂之前给予PTU，以抑制甲状腺利用即将到来的碘合成更多激素的能力。

byChoice.en: Propranolol is the immediate priority to reverse life-threatening sympathetic overstimulation (atrial fibrillation with RVR).
byChoice.zh: 普萘洛尔是首要任务，用于逆转危及生命的交感神经神经过度刺激（伴有RVR的房颤）。

byChoice.en: Hydrocortisone supports the adrenal axis during extreme stress and further decreases peripheral T4 to T3 conversion. It can be given concurrently or after the primary sequence is initiated.
byChoice.zh: 氢化可的松在极端压力下支持肾上腺轴，并进一步减少外周T4向T3的转化。它可以与主要顺序同时进行或在其启动后给予。

byChoice.en: Iodine is given 1-2 hours after PTU. It works by paralyzing the thyroid gland from releasing already-stored hormone.
byChoice.zh: 碘剂在PTU给药1-2小时后使用。其作用机制是麻痹甲状腺，阻止其释放已储存的激素。

testTakingStrategy.en: Memorize the sequence: Block the effects (Beta blocker) -> Block synthesis (PTU) -> Block release (Iodine) -> Block peripheral conversion (Steroids). Never give iodine before PTU.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 记住用药顺序：阻断效应（β受体阻滞剂）-> 阻断合成（PTU）-> 阻断释放（碘剂）-> 阻断外周转化（类固醇）。绝对不要在PTU之前给碘。

glossary.defZh: 一种非选择性β受体阻滞剂，用于减慢心率并减少交感神经的过度兴奋。
一种抗甲状腺药物，通过抑制过氧化物酶来阻止甲状腺激素的合成。

stem.en: To manage the client's hypermetabolic state and maintain safety between 0800 and 1200, which nursing interventions are indicated? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 为了在0800至1200之间控制患者的高代谢状态并维持安全，需要采取哪些护理干预措施？请选择所有适用项。

options.en: Administer prescribed aspirin for antipyretic relief.
options.zh: 遵医嘱给予阿司匹林以退热。

options.en: Apply a cooling blanket and ice packs to axillae and groin.
options.zh: 使用冰毯并将冰袋置于腋下和腹股沟。

options.en: Maintain a cool, dark, and quiet room environment.
options.zh: 维持凉爽、昏暗且安静的病房环境。

options.en: Administer prescribed acetaminophen for hyperthermia.
options.zh: 遵医嘱给予对乙酰氨基酚以治疗高热。

options.en: Restrict intravenous fluids to prevent pulmonary edema.
options.zh: 限制静脉输液以防止肺水肿。

options.en: Initiate seizure precautions.
options.zh: 启动癫痫预防措施。

correct.en: Nursing care for thyroid storm focuses on reducing hyperthermia, preventing injury from extreme agitation, and providing supportive care. Acetaminophen and physical cooling (cooling blankets, ice packs) are used aggressively to lower core temperature. A cool, dark, quiet environment minimizes sympathetic nervous system stimulation, reducing agitation and cardiac demand. Seizure precautions are necessary because severe hyperpyrexia and CNS hyper-excitability place the client at high risk for seizures. Aspirin is contraindicated because salicylates displace thyroid hormone from binding proteins, increasing free active thyroid hormone levels and worsening the storm. Fluid restriction is incorrect; these clients have profound insensible fluid losses from diaphoresis and hyperventilation and require aggressive IV fluid replacement.
correct.zh: 甲状腺危象的护理重点是降低高热、防止因极度烦躁引起的损伤并提供支持性护理。对乙酰氨基酚和物理降温（冰毯、冰袋）被积极用于降低核心体温。凉爽、昏暗、安静的环境可最大限度地减少交感神经系统刺激，从而减轻烦躁和心脏负荷。由于严重高热和中枢神经系统过度兴奋使患者处于癫痫发作的高危状态，因此必须采取癫痫预防措施。阿司匹林是禁忌的，因为水杨酸盐会将甲状腺激素从结合蛋白上置换下来，增加游离活性甲状腺激素水平并加重危象。限制输液是错误的；这些患者因大量出汗和过度通气会产生严重的不显性失水，需要积极的静脉补液。

byChoice.en: Aspirin (salicylates) is strictly contraindicated. It displaces thyroid hormones from carrier proteins, worsening the hypermetabolic state.
byChoice.zh: 阿司匹林（水杨酸盐）绝对禁忌。它会将甲状腺激素从载体蛋白上置换下来，从而加重高代谢状态。

byChoice.en: High fever and severe CNS irritability pose an imminent risk for seizures.
byChoice.zh: 高热和严重的中枢神经系统应激性构成癫痫发作的直接风险。

byChoice.en: External cooling methods are critical adjunctive therapies to rapidly decrease dangerous hyperpyrexia.
byChoice.zh: 体表降温方法是快速降低危险高热的关键辅助疗法。

byChoice.en: Limiting sensory stimulation decreases the sympathetic overdrive contributing to tachycardia and agitation.
byChoice.zh: 限制感官刺激可减少导致心动过速和烦躁的交感神经神经过度兴奋。

byChoice.en: Clients are typically severely dehydrated from extreme diaphoresis and hyperthermia; aggressive, not restricted, hydration is required.
byChoice.zh: 患者通常因大量出汗和高热而严重脱水；需要积极补水而非限制。

byChoice.en: Acetaminophen is the antipyretic of choice for thyroid storm as it effectively lowers temperature without affecting thyroid hormone binding.
byChoice.zh: 对乙酰氨基酚是甲状腺危象首选的退热药，因为它能在不影响甲状腺激素结合的情况下有效降温。

testTakingStrategy.en: When selecting medications for symptoms, always consider drug-disease interactions. Remember that aspirin is unsafe in thyroid storm. Treat the hypermetabolism with a calm environment and physical cooling.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在为症状选择药物时，请始终考虑药物与疾病的相互作用。记住阿司匹林在甲状腺危象中是不安全的。通过安静的环境和物理降温来应对高代谢。

glossary.defZh: 一类药物（如阿司匹林），在甲亢中应避免使用，因为它们会增加游离甲状腺激素。
难以测量的体液流失，主要通过皮肤出汗和呼吸道蒸发。

stem.en: The nurse evaluates the 1200 laboratory results to monitor for severe adverse effects of propylthiouracil (PTU). Which laboratory finding requires immediate notification to the healthcare provider?
stem.zh: 护士评估1200的实验室结果，以监测丙硫氧嘧啶 (PTU) 的严重不良反应。哪一项实验室发现需要立即通知医疗服务提供者？

options.en: Blood urea nitrogen (BUN) of 18 mg/dL (6.4 mmol/L)
options.zh: 血尿素氮 (BUN) 18 mg/dL (6.4 mmol/L)

options.en: Serum potassium of 3.8 mEq/L (3.8 mmol/L)
options.zh: 血清钾 3.8 mEq/L (3.8 mmol/L)

options.en: White blood cell count of 2,800/mm³ (2.8 × 10^9/L)
Free T4 of 4.2 ng/dL
options.zh: 游离T4 4.2 ng/dL

correct.en: A severe and potentially fatal adverse effect of propylthiouracil (PTU) and methimazole is agranulocytosis (a profound drop in white blood cells, specifically neutrophils). A total WBC count of 2,800/mm³ is abnormally low (leukopenia) and places the client at extreme risk for life-threatening sepsis, warranting immediate provider notification to stop the drug and implement reverse isolation. The potassium and BUN levels are within normal limits. The Free T4 remains elevated (normal 0.8-2.8 ng/dL); while abnormal, this is an expected finding at this stage, as it takes time for circulating hormone levels to fall even after synthesis and release are blocked.
correct.zh: 丙硫氧嘧啶 (PTU) 和甲巯咪唑的一种严重且具有潜在致命性的不良反应是粒细胞缺乏症（白细胞特别是中性粒细胞大幅下降）。2,800/mm³ 的白细胞总数异常偏低（白细胞减少症），使患者面临危及生命的败血症的极高风险，需要立即通知医生停药并实施保护性隔离。钾和BUN水平在正常范围内。游离T4仍然升高（正常值0.8-2.8 ng/dL）；虽然异常，但在这一阶段是预期的发现，因为即使合成和释放被阻断，循环中激素水平下降也需要时间。

byChoice.en: A BUN of 18 mg/dL is within the normal reference range (10-20 mg/dL).
byChoice.zh: BUN为 18 mg/dL 在正常参考范围 (10-20 mg/dL) 内。

byChoice.en: A serum potassium of 3.8 mEq/L is a normal physiological finding and does not indicate an adverse effect.
byChoice.zh: 血清钾 3.8 mEq/L 是正常的生理指标，不提示发生不良反应。

byChoice.en: An elevated free T4 is the cause of the storm; it is expected to remain elevated for days to weeks after starting PTU therapy as existing circulating levels metabolize.
byChoice.zh: 游离T4升高是危象的原因；在开始PTU治疗后的几天至几周内，随着现有循环水平的代谢，预计它仍将保持较高水平。

byChoice.en: Agranulocytosis/leukopenia is a life-threatening toxicity of PTU. The provider must be notified immediately to evaluate for drug discontinuation.
byChoice.zh: 粒细胞缺乏症/白细胞减少症是PTU危及生命的毒性反应。必须立即通知医生评估是否停药。

testTakingStrategy.en: When evaluating drug toxicity, look for the known severe complications. For antithyroid medications (PTU, methimazole), always monitor for signs of agranulocytosis (low WBC, fever, sore throat) and hepatotoxicity.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在评估药物毒性时，请寻找已知的严重并发症。对于抗甲状腺药物（PTU、甲巯咪唑），必须始终监测粒细胞缺乏症（白细胞低、发烧、喉咙痛）和肝毒性的迹象。

glossary.defZh: 一种严重的白细胞异常减少，导致发生严重感染的风险极高。
血液中白细胞数量低于正常水平。

correct.en: Thyroid storm requires immediate recognition and sequential intervention. Care begins with airway and cardiovascular stabilization (propranolol) to manage the deadly sympathetic surge. This is strictly followed by halting new thyroid hormone synthesis (PTU) before blocking its release (Lugol's iodine). Concurrently, nursing care focuses on aggressively lowering hyperpyrexia with safe methods (acetaminophen, cooling blankets, avoiding aspirin) and minimizing sensory stimulation. Finally, outcomes must be evaluated by monitoring both the resolution of symptoms (vital sign stability) and vigilance for severe pharmacological side effects, such as PTU-induced agranulocytosis.
correct.zh: 甲状腺危象需要立即识别并进行序贯干预。护理从气道和心血管稳定（普萘洛尔）开始，以控制致命的交感神经激增。这必须严格遵循先停止新甲状腺激素合成（PTU），然后再阻断其释放（Lugol液）的顺序。同时，护理工作侧重于使用安全方法（对乙酰氨基酚、冰毯、避免使用阿司匹林）积极降低高热，并将感觉刺激降至最低。最后，必须通过监测症状的缓解情况（生命体征稳定）和警惕严重的药理学副作用（如PTU引起的粒细胞缺乏症）来评估治疗结果。

testTakingStrategy.en: Unfolding cases require tracking clinical trends. Notice the timeline of medication administration and match nursing interventions to the physiological priorities at each stage (cardiac control -> source control -> complication prevention).
testTakingStrategy.zh: 进展性案例需要跟踪临床趋势。注意给药的时间线，并将护理干预措施与每个阶段的生理优先事项（心脏控制 -> 根源控制 -> 并发症预防）相匹配。

glossary.defZh: 一种罕见但危及生命的严重甲亢并发症，特征为高热、严重心动过速和意识改变。
一种严重的白细胞异常减少，导致发生严重感染的风险极高。
用于抑制甲状腺激素释放的含碘溶液，必须在抗甲状腺药物之后给药。

stem.en: After reviewing the client's record, answer the following questions. This case study addresses the management of a client with acute pulmonary edema.
stem.zh: 回顾客户记录后，请回答以下问题。本案例研究探讨了一名急性肺水肿客户的管理。

title.zh: 展开案例：急性呼吸窘迫

summary.en: A 74-year-old male with a history of chronic heart failure (ejection fraction 30%), hypertension, and atrial fibrillation presents to the emergency department with severe shortness of breath that started two hours ago. He states, "I feel like I'm drowning." He reports having to sleep in his recliner for the past two nights. His wife notes that he has not been taking his furosemide for the past three days because he "felt dehydrated."
summary.zh: 一名74岁男性，有慢性心力衰竭（射血分数为30%）、高血压和心房颤动病史，因两小时前开始的严重呼吸短促来到急诊科。他说：“我感觉自己像在溺水。”他报告说过去两晚都不得不在躺椅上睡觉。他的妻子指出，过去三天他没有服用呋塞米，因为他“感觉脱水了”。

content.en:
Vital Signs:
- Temperature: 36.8°C (98.2°F)
- Heart Rate: 128 beats/minute, irregularly irregular
- Respiratory Rate: 34 breaths/minute, labored
- Blood Pressure: 188/102 mmHg
- SpO2: 85% on room air

Assessment:
- General: Anxious, sitting upright in tripod position, using accessory muscles.
- Respiratory: Diffuse bilateral crackles halfway up the lung fields.
- Cardiovascular: S3 gallop present. 2+ pitting edema in bilateral lower extremities.
- Skin: Pale and diaphoretic.
content.zh:
生命体征：
- 体温：36.8°C (98.2°F)
- 心率：128次/分钟，不规则且不规律
- 呼吸频率：34次/分钟，费力
- 血压：188/102 mmHg
- 血氧饱和度：室内空气下为85%

评估：
- 一般情况：焦虑，呈三脚架体位坐立，使用辅助呼吸肌。
- 呼吸系统：双肺野弥漫性湿啰音，高达肺野中部。
- 心血管系统：可闻及S3奔马律。双下肢2+凹陷性水肿。
- 皮肤：苍白、大汗。

stem.en: Which findings from the initial assessment are most indicative of acute decompensated heart failure with pulmonary edema? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 初步评估中的哪些发现最能表明患有急性失代偿性心力衰竭伴肺水肿？请选择所有适用项。

options.en: Tripod positioning
options.zh: 三脚架体位

options.en: SpO2 of 85% on room air
options.zh: 室内空气下血氧饱和度为85%

options.en: S3 gallop
options.zh: S3奔马律

options.en: Bilateral crackles
options.zh: 双侧湿啰音

options.en: Blood pressure of 188/102 mmHg
options.zh: 血压188/102 mmHg

options.en: History of atrial fibrillation
options.zh: 心房颤动病史

correct.en: The classic signs of acute pulmonary edema, a life-threatening manifestation of ADHF, include severe dyspnea (leading to tripod positioning), hypoxemia (low SpO2), and diffuse crackles on auscultation due to fluid in the alveoli. An S3 gallop is a hallmark sign of fluid volume overload and failing left ventricular function. While hypertension is a common precipitating factor and atrial fibrillation is a common comorbidity, they are not as specific to the acute fluid shift into the lungs as the other findings.
correct.zh: 急性肺水肿是ADHF危及生命的表现，其典型体征包括严重呼吸困难（导致三脚架体位）、低氧血症（低SpO2）以及因肺泡积液而在听诊时闻及的弥漫性湿啰音。 S3奔马律是液体容量超负荷和左心室功能衰竭的标志性体征。虽然高血压是常见的诱发因素，心房颤动是常见的合并症，但它们不像其他发现那样对肺部急性液体转移具有特异性。

byChoice.en: Severe hypertension can be a cause of ADHF (hypertensive emergency leading to increased afterload), but it is not a direct sign of fluid in the lungs itself. Some patients may present with hypotension.
byChoice.zh: 严重高血压可能是ADHF的原因（高血压急症导致后负荷增加），但它不是肺部积液本身的直接迹象。一些患者可能表现为低血压。

byChoice.en: The tripod position is an attempt by the client to maximize lung expansion and decrease the work of breathing in the setting of severe respiratory distress, which is characteristic of acute pulmonary edema.
byChoice.zh: 三脚架体位是客户在严重呼吸窘迫的情况下，为最大限度地扩大肺部并减少呼吸功而采取的姿势，这是急性肺水肿的特征。

byChoice.en: Atrial fibrillation is a risk factor and can precipitate an exacerbation by reducing diastolic filling time, but it is part of the client's history, not a direct physical sign of pulmonary edema.
byChoice.zh: 心房颤动是一个风险因素，可通过减少舒张期充盈时间而诱发病情加重，但它属于客户的病史，而不是肺水肿的直接体征。

byChoice.en: An S3 heart sound in an adult is an early diastolic sound indicating a large volume of blood flowing into a compliant ventricle, a classic sign of volume overload in heart failure.
byChoice.zh: 成年人中的S3心音是舒张早期的声音，表明大量血液流入顺应性好的心室，是心力衰竭中容量超负荷的典型体征。

byChoice.en: Crackles are adventitious breath sounds caused by the opening of small airways and alveoli collapsed by fluid, which is the defining feature of pulmonary edema.
byChoice.zh: 湿啰音是由于被液体压闭的小气道和肺泡重新张开而产生的附加呼吸音，这是肺水肿的决定性特征。

byChoice.en: Severe hypoxemia reflects impaired gas exchange at the alveolar-capillary membrane due to fluid accumulation, a direct consequence of pulmonary edema.
byChoice.zh: 严重低氧血症反映了由于液体积聚导致的肺泡-毛细血管膜气体交换受损，这是肺水肿的直接后果。

testTakingStrategy.en: Focus on the most specific signs of the condition named in the question (pulmonary edema). Ask, 'Which of these findings directly points to fluid in the lungs or the heart's inability to handle volume?' A history item or a potential cause is less specific than a direct physical assessment finding.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 关注问题中提到的病症（肺水肿）的最具体体征。问问自己，“这些发现中，哪一个直接指向肺部积液或心脏无法处理容量？”病史项目或潜在原因不如直接的体格检查发现具体。

glossary.defZh: 由于肺血管内压力增高导致液体渗出到肺间质和肺泡内，引起严重的呼吸困难和气体交换障碍。
在心动周期舒张早期出现的额外心音，通常与心室快速充盈和容量超负荷有关。

stem.en: The nurse needs to take immediate action to stabilize the client. Place the following nursing interventions in the correct order of priority.
stem.zh: 护士需要立即采取行动稳定客户。请将以下护理干预措施按正确的优先顺序排列。

options.en: Place the client in a high-Fowler's position.
options.zh: 将客户置于高福勒卧位。

options.en: Apply supplemental oxygen via a non-rebreather mask.
options.zh: 通过非再呼吸面罩给予补充氧气。

options.en: Administer intravenous furosemide.
options.zh: 静脉注射呋塞米。

options.en: Establish intravenous access.
options.zh: 建立静脉通路。

correct.en: The immediate priorities are to improve oxygenation and reduce the work of breathing. Placing the client upright (high-Fowler's) immediately reduces venous return to the heart and allows for better lung expansion. Applying oxygen addresses the severe hypoxemia. Establishing IV access is necessary before administering IV medications. Finally, administering a potent loop diuretic like furosemide is critical to remove excess fluid, but this requires IV access and follows the immediate stabilization of breathing.
correct.zh: 当务之急是改善氧合和减少呼吸功。将客户置于直立位（高福勒卧位）可立即减少回心血量，并有助于更好的肺扩张。 给予氧气可解决严重的低氧血症。 在给予静脉药物之前，必须建立静脉通路。最后，给予像呋塞米这样的强效袢利尿剂对于清除多余液体至关重要，但这需要静脉通路，并且是在呼吸初步稳定之后进行。

byChoice.en: Intravenous access is a prerequisite for administering essential medications like diuretics and vasodilators. This is a necessary preparatory step before definitive pharmacologic treatment can begin.
byChoice.zh: 静脉通路是给予利尿剂和血管扩张剂等基本药物的先决条件。这是在开始确定性药物治疗之前必须的准备步骤。

byChoice.en: Correcting severe hypoxemia (SpO2 85%) is a critical life-saving measure. A non-rebreather mask delivers a high concentration of oxygen to address the significant ventilation-perfusion mismatch.
byChoice.zh: 纠正严重低氧血症（SpO2 85%）是一项关键的救生措施。非再呼吸面罩可提供高浓度的氧气，以解决显著的通气-灌注不匹配问题。

byChoice.en: Positioning the client upright is the fastest, non-invasive intervention to decrease preload by allowing fluid to pool in the lower extremities and improve diaphragmatic excursion, providing immediate relief.
byChoice.zh: 将客户置于直立位是最快、无创的干预措施，通过让液体积聚在下肢来降低前负荷，并改善膈肌运动，从而立即缓解症状。

byChoice.en: Administering IV furosemide is a key intervention to promote diuresis and reduce total body fluid volume, but it can only be done after IV access is secured and the client's immediate oxygenation needs are being addressed.
byChoice.zh: 给予静脉注射呋塞米是促进利尿和减少全身液体量的关键干预措施，但这只能在确保静脉通路并解决客户的即时氧合需求后才能进行。

testTakingStrategy.en: In acute respiratory distress, always prioritize the least invasive and fastest-acting interventions first. Positioning and oxygen are immediate. Then, prepare for medications (IV access) before administering them. Think 'Airway, Breathing, Circulation' but apply it practically: improve breathing with position/O2, then address circulation with meds.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在急性呼吸窘迫中，始终优先考虑侵入性最小、起效最快的干预措施。体位和氧气是立即可行的。然后，在给药前准备好药物（静脉通路）。思考“气道、呼吸、循环”，但要实际应用：通过体位/氧气改善呼吸，然后用药物处理循环问题。

glossary.defZh: 一种半坐卧位，床头抬高60-90度，有助于缓解呼吸困难和减少回心血量。
一种能提供高浓度氧气（可达90%以上）的氧疗设备，用于严重低氧血症的患者。

stem.en: The nurse is formulating a clinical hypothesis. Complete the following sentence by choosing from the dropdown options.
stem.zh: 护士正在形成一个临床假设。请从下拉选项中选择，完成以下句子。

clozeStem.en: The client is most likely experiencing {{1}} as a result of {{2}}.
clozeStem.zh: 该客户最有可能正在经历 {{1}}，这是由于 {{2}} 的结果。

options.en: a pulmonary embolism
options.zh: 肺栓塞

options.en: cardiogenic pulmonary edema
options.zh: 心源性肺水肿

options.en: an acute asthma exacerbation
options.zh: 急性哮喘发作

options.en: an allergic reaction
options.zh: 过敏反应

options.en: medication non-adherence
options.zh: 药物不依从性

options.en: a primary respiratory infection
options.zh: 原发性呼吸道感染

correct.en: The client's history of severe heart failure, combined with classic signs of fluid overload (S3, edema, crackles, orthopnea) and the reported non-adherence with his diuretic medication, strongly supports the diagnosis of cardiogenic pulmonary edema. The lack of fever or productive cough makes infection less likely, and the bilateral nature of the findings is not typical for a pulmonary embolism.
correct.zh: 客户的严重心力衰竭病史，结合液体超负荷的典型体征（S3、水肿、湿啰音、端坐呼吸）以及报告的利尿剂药物不依从性，有力地支持了心源性肺水肿的诊断。没有发烧或咳痰使得感染的可能性较小，而发现的双侧性特征对于肺栓塞来说并不典型。

byChoice.en: Cardiogenic pulmonary edema is the correct hypothesis, as it is caused by increased pressure in the heart's left ventricle, which is consistent with the client's history and physical exam findings.
byChoice.zh: 心源性肺水肿是正确的假设，因为它是由心脏左心室压力增高引起的，这与客户的病史和体格检查结果一致。

byChoice.en: Medication non-adherence, specifically stopping a diuretic, is a very common precipitant for ADHF, as it leads to rapid fluid retention and volume overload in a patient with underlying cardiac dysfunction.
byChoice.zh: 药物不依从性，特别是停用利尿剂，是ADHF一个非常常见的诱因，因为它会导致有潜在心脏功能障碍的患者迅速出现液体潴留和容量超负荷。

testTakingStrategy.en: For cloze items, evaluate each dropdown independently first, then read the completed sentence to ensure it makes clinical sense. Use the case data to support your choices. The client's history (CHF) and the precipitating event (stopped furosemide) are the key clues here.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 对于完形填空题，首先独立评估每个下拉选项，然后阅读完整的句子以确保其在临床上合理。使用案例数据来支持您的选择。客户的病史（CHF）和诱发事件（停用呋塞米）是这里的关键线索。

glossary.defZh: 指起源于心脏或由心脏功能障碍引起的。
患者遵循医疗服务提供者推荐的用药方案的程度。

stem.en: After administering 80 mg of intravenous furosemide, which assessment finding would be the most reliable indicator that the medication has been effective?
stem.zh: 在静脉注射80毫克呋塞米后，哪项评估发现是表明药物有效的最可靠指标？

options.en: The client's heart rate decreases to 110 beats/minute.
options.zh: 客户的心率降至110次/分钟。

options.en: The client has a urine output of 400 mL in the first hour.
options.zh: 客户在第一个小时内的尿量为400毫升。

options.en: The client's blood pressure decreases to 150/88 mmHg.
options.zh: 客户的血压降至150/88 mmHg。

options.en: The client reports a decrease in anxiety.
options.zh: 客户报告焦虑减轻。

correct.en: Furosemide is a loop diuretic that works by inhibiting water reabsorption in the nephron, leading to significant diuresis. The primary therapeutic effect in pulmonary edema is the rapid removal of excess fluid from the body. Therefore, a brisk and significant increase in urine output is the most direct and reliable indicator of the drug's effectiveness. While other parameters may improve as a consequence of diuresis, they are secondary effects.
correct.zh: 呋塞米是一种袢利尿剂，通过抑制肾单位对水的重吸收而起作用，导致显著的利尿。在肺水肿中，其主要治疗效果是快速清除体内多余的液体。因此，尿量迅速而显著的增加是药物有效性的最直接、最可靠的指标。虽然其他参数可能因利尿而改善，但它们是次要效应。

byChoice.en: A decrease in anxiety is a positive but subjective finding. It is likely a result of improved breathing, which is a consequence of the diuretic working, but it is not the most objective measure of the drug's primary effect.
byChoice.zh: 焦虑减轻是一个积极但主观的发现。这很可能是呼吸改善的结果，而呼吸改善是利尿剂起作用的后果，但它不是药物主要作用的最客观衡量标准。

byChoice.en: A decrease in heart rate is a positive sign, likely reflecting reduced stress on the heart as fluid volume decreases. However, like blood pressure, it is a secondary indicator of improved overall status, not a direct measure of the diuretic's action.
byChoice.zh: 心率下降是一个积极的迹象，可能反映了随着液体容量减少，心脏压力减轻。然而，与血压一样，它是整体状况改善的次要指标，而不是利尿剂作用的直接衡量标准。

byChoice.en: A decrease in blood pressure is expected due to volume removal, but it is a secondary effect and can also be influenced by other medications (like vasodilators) or the client's condition. It is not the most direct measure of diuretic action.
byChoice.zh: 由于容量减少，血压下降是预期的，但这是一个次要效应，也可能受到其他药物（如血管扩张剂）或客户状况的影响。它不是利尿作用的最直接衡量标准。

byChoice.en: A large volume of urine output (diuresis) is the direct pharmacological action of furosemide. This finding confirms the drug is working as intended to remove excess fluid, which is the primary goal of therapy in this situation.
byChoice.zh: 大量的尿量输出（利尿）是呋塞米的直接药理作用。这一发现证实了药物正在按预期工作以清除多余的液体，这是在这种情况下治疗的主要目标。

testTakingStrategy.en: When evaluating the effectiveness of a medication, focus on its primary mechanism of action. What is the drug supposed to do? For a diuretic, the answer is 'produce urine.' Look for the option that directly measures this primary effect.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在评估药物的有效性时，请关注其主要作用机制。这种药应该做什么？对于利尿剂，答案是“产生尿液”。寻找直接衡量这种主要效果的选项。

glossary.defZh: 一种强效的袢利尿剂，通过抑制肾脏对盐和水的重吸收来迅速增加尿量。
尿液生成和排泄增加的过程。

correct.en: This case study focuses on the assessment and immediate management of acute decompensated heart failure presenting as pulmonary edema. It requires the nurse to recognize critical cues, prioritize life-saving interventions based on pathophysiology, formulate a correct clinical hypothesis, and evaluate the direct effect of pharmacological therapy.
correct.zh: 本案例研究侧重于评估和即时管理表现为肺水肿的急性失代偿性心力衰竭。它要求护士识别关键线索，根据病理生理学优先安排救生干预措施，形成正确的临床假设，并评估药物治疗的直接效果。

testTakingStrategy.en: For high-acuity respiratory cases, always follow the sequence of assess, intervene, and evaluate. Prioritize interventions that provide the most immediate benefit to oxygenation and hemodynamics. When evaluating medication effects, link the assessment finding directly to the drug's mechanism of action.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 对于高危呼吸系统病例，始终遵循评估、干预和评价的顺序。优先考虑对氧合和血流动力学提供最直接益处的干预措施。在评估药物效果时，将评估发现与药物的作用机制直接联系起来。

questions.topic: Acute Myocardial Infarction and Ventricular Fibrillation

stem.en: Carefully review the provided clinical documentation and answer the unfolding case study questions regarding this critical cardiovascular emergency.
stem.zh: 请仔细查阅提供的临床记录，并回答有关这一危急心血管急症的展开式案例研究问题。

title.zh: 展开案例：突发心脏骤停

summary.en: A 62-year-old female client is brought to the emergency department by emergency medical services after experiencing sudden, crushing substernal chest pain that radiates to her left jaw and arm. She is visibly diaphoretic, clutching her chest, and reports severe nausea and shortness of breath.
summary.zh: 一名62岁女性客户在经历突然的、压榨性的胸骨后疼痛（放射至左下颌和手臂）后，由紧急医疗服务送至急诊科。她明显大汗淋漓，紧捂胸口，并报告有严重的恶心和呼吸短促。

content.en:
Vital Signs:
- Blood Pressure: 155/90 mmHg
- Heart Rate: 110 beats/minute
- Respiratory Rate: 28 breaths/minute
- SpO2: 92% on room air

Diagnostic Testing:
- 12-Lead ECG: Reveals 4 mm ST-segment elevation in leads V1 through V4.
- Troponin I: Pending.
content.zh:
生命体征：
- 血压：155/90 mmHg
- 心率：110次/分钟
- 呼吸频率：28次/分钟
- 血氧饱和度：室内空气下为92%

诊断测试：
- 12导联心电图：显示导联 V1 至 V4 有 4 mm 的 ST 段抬高。
- 肌钙蛋白 I：待出。

content.en: While the nurse is preparing the client for immediate transport to the cardiac catheterization laboratory, the client's eyes roll back, and she becomes completely unresponsive. The cardiac monitor alarms, displaying a chaotic, irregular, and rapid waveform with no discernible P waves, QRS complexes, or T waves. The client has no palpable carotid pulse.
content.zh: 当护士正准备将客户立即转运至心导管室时，客户双眼上翻，变得完全无意识。心电监护仪报警，显示出混乱、不规则且快速的波形，没有可辨认的 P 波、QRS 波群或 T 波。客户颈动脉搏动消失。

stem.en: Based on the initial triage assessment, which of the following clinical findings strongly suggest an acute myocardial infarction accompanied by sympathetic nervous system activation? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 根据初步分诊评估，以下哪些临床发现强烈提示急性心肌梗死伴有交感神经系统激活？请选择所有适用项。

options.en: A resting heart rate of 110 beats/minute.
options.zh: 静息心率为 110 次/分钟。

options.en: ST-segment elevation in leads V1 through V4.
options.zh: 导联 V1 至 V4 的 ST 段抬高。

options.en: Visible, profuse diaphoresis.
options.zh: 可见的、大量的大汗淋漓。

options.en: An oxygen saturation of 92% on room air.
options.zh: 室内空气下血氧饱和度为 92%。

options.en: Crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the jaw.
options.zh: 压榨性胸骨后疼痛放射至下颌。

correct.en: Crushing chest pain radiating to the jaw is the classic ischemic symptom of a myocardial infarction. ST-segment elevation in V1-V4 provides definitive electrocardiographic evidence of an acute anterior wall STEMI. Diaphoresis and tachycardia (HR 110) are direct results of the body's massive sympathetic nervous system (fight-or-flight) response to severe pain and decreased cardiac output. While the SpO2 of 92% is slightly low and warrants oxygen therapy, it is a secondary finding of mild hypoxia, not a direct indicator of sympathetic activation or the infarction itself.
correct.zh: 放射至下颌的压榨性胸痛是心肌梗死的典型缺血症状。V1-V4 的 ST 段抬高提供了急性前壁 STEMI 的确切心电图证据。大汗淋漓和心动过速（心率 110）是身体对剧烈疼痛和心输出量减少产生的大量交感神经系统（战斗或逃跑）反应的直接结果。虽然 92% 的 SpO2 略低且需要氧疗，但它是轻度缺氧的次要发现，而不是交感神经激活或梗死本身的直接指标。

byChoice.en: Tachycardia (HR 110) is a compensatory mechanism driven by the sympathetic nervous system attempting to maintain cardiac output in the face of impaired ventricular pumping.
byChoice.zh: 心动过速（心率 110）是由交感神经系统驱动的代偿机制，试图在心室泵血功能受损的情况下维持心输出量。

byChoice.en: Profuse diaphoresis (sweating) is a classic sign of sympathetic nervous system activation, triggered by the severe stress and pain of the heart attack.
byChoice.zh: 大量出汗（大汗淋漓）是交感神经系统激活的典型体征，由心脏病发作的严重压力和疼痛引发。

byChoice.en: Crushing, radiating chest pain is the hallmark symptom of myocardial ischemia, caused by the buildup of lactic acid in heart tissue deprived of oxygen.
byChoice.zh: 压榨性、放射性胸痛是心肌缺血的标志性症状，由缺氧的心脏组织中乳酸积聚引起。

byChoice.en: A slightly low oxygen saturation (92%) indicates mild hypoxemia, likely due to poor perfusion or early pulmonary congestion, but it is not a direct sign of sympathetic activation.
byChoice.zh: 略低的血氧饱和度（92%）表明轻度低氧血症，可能是由于灌注不良或早期肺淤血引起的，但它不是交感神经激活的直接体征。

byChoice.en: ST-segment elevation in leads V1-V4 is the definitive diagnostic cue for an acute anterior wall ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction (STEMI), indicating full-thickness heart muscle injury.
byChoice.zh: 导联 V1-V4 的 ST 段抬高是急性前壁 ST 段抬高型心肌梗死 (STEMI) 的明确诊断线索，表明全层心肌损伤。

testTakingStrategy.en: Break the question into two parts: signs of an MI AND signs of sympathetic activation. Chest pain and ST elevation confirm the MI. Sweating and a fast heart rate confirm the sympathetic 'fight-or-flight' adrenaline surge that accompanies the severe stress of a heart attack.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 将问题分为两部分：心肌梗死的体征和交感神经激活的体征。胸痛和 ST 段抬高证实了心肌梗死。出汗和心率加快证实了伴随心脏病发作严重压力的交感神经“战斗或逃跑”肾上腺素激增。

glossary.defZh: 一种严重的心脏病发作，冠状动脉完全阻塞，导致心肌全层损伤，在心电图上表现为 ST 段抬高。
自主神经系统的一部分，负责身体的“战斗或逃跑”反应，导致心率加快、血压升高和出汗。

stem.en: The client suddenly becomes unresponsive with a chaotic rhythm on the monitor. Arrange the following emergency nursing actions in the most appropriate sequential order.
stem.zh: 客户突然变得无意识，监护仪上显示混乱的节律。请按最合适的顺序排列以下紧急护理操作。

options.en: Prepare to administer intravenous epinephrine 1 mg.
options.zh: 准备静脉注射 1 mg 肾上腺素。

options.en: Call for the resuscitation team and a defibrillator.
options.zh: 呼叫复苏团队并要求提供除颤器。

options.en: Deliver an unsynchronized electrical shock (defibrillation).
options.zh: 进行非同步电击（除颤）。

options.en: Initiate high-quality cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) starting with chest compressions.
options.zh: 开始高质量的心肺复苏 (CPR)，从胸外按压开始。

correct.en: According to Basic Life Support (BLS) and Advanced Cardiovascular Life Support (ACLS) guidelines, the first action upon discovering an unresponsive pulseless client is to call for help and a defibrillator. Immediately after calling for help, the nurse must start high-quality CPR (chest compressions) to maintain minimal perfusion to the brain and heart. As soon as the defibrillator arrives and ventricular fibrillation is confirmed, an unsynchronized shock must be delivered, as early defibrillation is the only definitive treatment for V-Fib. Following the shock, CPR is immediately resumed, and pharmacological interventions like epinephrine are prepared and administered.
correct.zh: 根据基础生命支持 (BLS) 和高级心血管生命支持 (ACLS) 指南，发现无意识且无脉搏的客户时，第一步操作是呼救并要求提供除颤器。呼救后，护士必须立即开始高质量的 CPR（胸外按压），以维持大脑和心脏的最低灌注。一旦除颤器到达并确认是心室颤动，必须立即进行非同步电击，因为早期除颤是治疗室颤的唯一确切方法。电击后，立即恢复 CPR，并准备和施用肾上腺素等药物干预措施。

byChoice.en: High-quality chest compressions must begin immediately after calling for help to manually pump blood to vital organs while waiting for the defibrillator to be charged and ready.
byChoice.zh: 在呼救后必须立即开始高质量的胸外按压，以在等待除颤器充电和准备就绪期间，手动将血液泵入重要器官。

byChoice.en: Calling for help and the defibrillator is always the first step. You cannot successfully resuscitate a patient in V-Fib alone without the proper equipment and team.
byChoice.zh: 呼救和要求提供除颤器始终是第一步。如果没有适当的设备和团队，您无法独自成功复苏室颤患者。

byChoice.en: Medications like epinephrine are administered during CPR after the initial shock(s) have been delivered. They support the resuscitation effort but do not replace the immediate need for electricity in V-Fib.
byChoice.zh: 肾上腺素等药物是在进行初始电击后的 CPR 期间给药的。它们支持复苏工作，但不能取代室颤对电击的迫切需求。

byChoice.en: Defibrillation is the definitive treatment for ventricular fibrillation. It should be performed as soon as the machine is attached and the shockable rhythm is identified.
byChoice.zh: 除颤是心室颤动的确切治疗方法。一旦连接好机器并确认了可电击心律，就应尽快执行。

testTakingStrategy.en: Follow the strict AHA BLS/ACLS algorithm for a witnessed cardiac arrest: 1. Recognize/Call for help. 2. Start CPR. 3. Defibrillate (for VF/pVT). 4. Medications (Epinephrine/Amiodarone). Never delay CPR or defibrillation to draw up medications.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 遵循美国心脏协会 (AHA) BLS/ACLS 针对目击心脏骤停的严格算法：1. 识别/呼救。2. 开始 CPR。3. 除颤（针对室颤/无脉性室速）。4. 药物治疗（肾上腺素/胺碘酮）。切勿为了抽取药物而延迟 CPR 或除颤。

glossary.defZh: 一种致命的心律失常，心室发生快速、不规则的颤动，无法泵血，导致心脏骤停。
向心脏传递治疗剂量的电能，以终止心室颤动并使心脏恢复正常节律的过程。

stem.en: The resuscitation team is utilizing multiple pharmacological agents during the cardiac arrest and peri-arrest period. Match each medication to its primary pharmacological classification.
stem.zh: 复苏团队在心脏骤停及围骤停期使用了多种药物。请将每种药物与其主要的药理学分类相匹配。

rows.en: Amiodarone
rows.zh: 胺碘酮

rows.en: Epinephrine
rows.zh: 肾上腺素

rows.en: Aspirin
rows.zh: 阿司匹林

rows.en: Nitroglycerin
rows.zh: 硝酸甘油

correct.en: Amiodarone is a class III antiarrhythmic used in ACLS to treat refractory ventricular fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia. Epinephrine is a potent vasopressor (alpha- and beta-agonist) given during cardiac arrest to increase aortic diastolic pressure and coronary perfusion. Aspirin is an antiplatelet agent given early in acute coronary syndrome to prevent further clot aggregation in the coronary arteries. Nitroglycerin is a vasodilator used to reduce preload and myocardial oxygen demand, relieving ischemic chest pain.
correct.zh: 胺碘酮是 ACLS 中用于治疗难治性心室颤动和室性心动过速的 III 类抗心律失常药。肾上腺素是一种强效血管加压药（α 和 β 激动剂），在心脏骤停期间给药以增加主动脉舒张压和冠状动脉灌注。阿司匹林是一种抗血小板药，在急性冠脉综合征早期给药，以防止冠状动脉内血栓进一步聚集。硝酸甘油是一种血管扩张剂，用于降低前负荷和心肌耗氧量，缓解缺血性胸痛。

byChoice.en: Amiodarone prolongs the cardiac action potential and refractory period. It is the primary antiarrhythmic medication used in the ACLS algorithm for shock-refractory ventricular fibrillation.
byChoice.zh: 胺碘酮延长心脏动作电位和不应期。它是 ACLS 算法中用于治疗电击难治性心室颤动的主要抗心律失常药物。

byChoice.en: Epinephrine's primary benefit in cardiac arrest is its alpha-adrenergic (vasopressor) effect, which causes intense peripheral vasoconstriction, shunting blood to the heart and brain during CPR.
byChoice.zh: 肾上腺素在心脏骤停中的主要益处是其 α-肾上腺素能（血管加压）作用，它引起强烈的外周血管收缩，在 CPR 期间将血液分流至心脏和大脑。

byChoice.en: Aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation. It is a critical early intervention in acute myocardial infarction to stop the growth of the thrombus blocking the coronary artery.
byChoice.zh: 阿司匹林抑制血小板聚集。它是急性心肌梗死中阻止阻塞冠状动脉的血栓生长的关键早期干预措施。

byChoice.en: Nitroglycerin relaxes vascular smooth muscle, causing vasodilation. This decreases venous return to the heart (preload), reducing the workload and oxygen demand of the ischemic myocardium.
byChoice.zh: 硝酸甘油松弛血管平滑肌，引起血管扩张。这减少了回心血量（前负荷），降低了缺血心肌的工作负荷和耗氧量。

testTakingStrategy.en: Group cardiac medications by their primary mechanism. Epinephrine squeezes vessels (pressor). Nitro opens vessels (dilator). Aspirin makes blood slippery (antiplatelet). Amiodarone fixes the electrical rhythm (antiarrhythmic).
testTakingStrategy.zh: 按主要机制对心脏药物进行分组。肾上腺素挤压血管（加压药）。硝酸甘油打开血管（扩张剂）。阿司匹林使血液变滑（抗血小板药）。胺碘酮修复电节律（抗心律失常药）。

glossary.defZh: 用于预防或治疗心脏节律异常（如心室颤动）的药物。
使血管扩张的药物，从而降低血压并减少心脏的工作负荷。

stem.en: During the resuscitation effort, the nurse continuously monitors the client's physiological responses. Which specific clinical finding provides the most definitive evidence of the return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC)?
stem.zh: 在复苏过程中，护士持续监测客户的生理反应。哪项具体的临床发现提供了恢复自主循环 (ROSC) 的最确凿证据？

options.en: The appearance of an organized sinus rhythm on the cardiac monitor without a palpable pulse.
options.zh: 心电监护仪上出现有组织的窦性心律，但无触及脉搏。

options.en: Bilateral pupillary dilation and unresponsiveness to light.
options.zh: 双侧瞳孔散大且对光无反应。

options.en: The presence of occasional, irregular gasping respirations (agonal breathing).
options.zh: 出现偶尔的、不规则的喘息样呼吸（濒死呼吸）。

options.en: An abrupt and sustained increase in PETCO2 (end-tidal carbon dioxide) to 40 mmHg.
options.zh: PETCO2（呼气末二氧化碳）突然且持续地增加至 40 mmHg。

correct.en: An abrupt and sustained increase in end-tidal carbon dioxide (PETCO2), typically to a normal value of 35-40 mmHg, is the earliest and most reliable non-invasive indicator of Return of Spontaneous Circulation (ROSC). During cardiac arrest, PETCO2 is low because blood is not circulating to the lungs to release CO2. When the heart restarts and cardiac output is restored, accumulated CO2 is suddenly washed out of the tissues and delivered to the lungs, causing a sharp spike on the capnography monitor. This often occurs even before a pulse can be reliably palpated.
correct.zh: 呼气末二氧化碳 (PETCO2) 突然且持续地增加（通常达到 35-40 mmHg 的正常值）是恢复自主循环 (ROSC) 最早、最可靠的无创指标。在心脏骤停期间，PETCO2 很低，因为血液没有循环到肺部释放 CO2。当心脏重新启动并恢复心输出量时，积聚的 CO2 突然从组织中被冲刷出来并输送到肺部，导致二氧化碳波形图监护仪上出现急剧的峰值。这通常甚至在能够可靠地触及脉搏之前发生。

byChoice.en: Fixed and dilated pupils indicate severe cerebral hypoxia and potential brain death. This is a negative prognostic sign during resuscitation, not an indicator of restored circulation.
byChoice.zh: 瞳孔固定和散大表明严重的脑缺氧和潜在的脑死亡。这是复苏过程中的不良预后体征，而不是循环恢复的指标。

byChoice.en: An organized rhythm on the monitor without a palpable pulse is the definition of Pulseless Electrical Activity (PEA). It means the electrical system is working, but the mechanical pump is failing. This is not ROSC; CPR must continue.
byChoice.zh: 监护仪上有组织的节律但无触及脉搏是无脉性电活动 (PEA) 的定义。这意味着电气系统正在工作，但机械泵发生故障。这不是 ROSC；必须继续进行 CPR。

byChoice.en: Agonal gasps are brainstem reflexes that can occur early in cardiac arrest. They are not effective breathing and do not indicate that the heart has restarted pumping blood.
byChoice.zh: 濒死喘息是可能在心脏骤停早期发生的脑干反射。它们不是有效的呼吸，也不表明心脏已重新开始泵血。

byChoice.en: This is the correct answer. A sudden spike in PETCO2 indicates that pulmonary blood flow has been restored by a beating heart, allowing carbon dioxide to be exhaled. It is the gold standard indicator of ROSC during CPR.
byChoice.zh: 这是正确答案。PETCO2 的突然飙升表明跳动的心脏恢复了肺血流，使得二氧化碳能够被呼出。它是 CPR 期间 ROSC 的黄金标准指标。

testTakingStrategy.en: Remember that the monitor lies, but perfusion does not. An ECG rhythm does not guarantee a pulse (PEA). However, a sudden jump in exhaled CO2 (PETCO2) proves that blood is actively moving from the tissues to the lungs, which only happens if the heart is pumping.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 请记住，监护仪会说谎，但灌注不会。心电图节律不能保证有脉搏 (PEA)。然而，呼出 CO2 (PETCO2) 的突然跳跃证明血液正在积极地从组织流向肺部，这只有在心脏泵血时才会发生。

glossary.defZh: 在心脏骤停后，心脏恢复持续的泵血活动，表现为可触及的脉搏、血压恢复和呼气末二氧化碳突然升高。
呼气末期呼出气体中二氧化碳的浓度，用于在心肺复苏期间监测心输出量和肺血流。

correct.en: This unfolding case study tests the nurse's ability to recognize the classic presentation of an acute myocardial infarction, anticipate its most lethal complication (ventricular fibrillation), and execute the strict, time-sensitive ACLS algorithms for defibrillation and pharmacological resuscitation while accurately evaluating the physiological markers of success.
correct.zh: 这个展开式案例研究测试了护士识别急性心肌梗死典型表现的能力，预期其最致命并发症（心室颤动）的能力，以及执行严格的、对时间敏感的 ACLS 除颤和药物复苏算法的能力，同时准确评估成功的生理标志物。

testTakingStrategy.en: In cardiac arrest scenarios, prioritize actions that directly restore perfusion. For V-Fib, electricity (defibrillation) is the cure, and CPR buys time. Medications are secondary. Always look for objective signs of perfusion (like PETCO2 or a palpable pulse) to confirm ROSC, not just a rhythm on the screen.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在心脏骤停场景中，优先考虑直接恢复灌注的操作。对于室颤，电击（除颤）是治愈方法，而 CPR 争取时间。药物是次要的。始终寻找灌注的客观体征（如 PETCO2 或可触及的脉搏）来确认 ROSC，而不仅仅是屏幕上的节律。

stem.en: After reviewing the client's record, answer the following questions. This case study follows a client's progression from initial presentation to critical intervention.
stem.zh: 回顾客户记录后，请回答以下问题。本案例研究追踪了一名客户从初次就诊到关键干预的进展过程。

title.en: Unfolding Case: Deterioration in an Older Adult
title.zh: 展开案例：一名老年成年人的病情恶化

summary.en: An 82-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and recurrent urinary tract infections (UTIs) is brought to the emergency department by her daughter, who reports the client has been increasingly confused and lethargic over the past 24 hours. The daughter also notes the client has had a decreased urine output and has not been eating or drinking well.
summary.zh: 一名82岁女性，有2型糖尿病和复发性尿路感染（UTI）病史，由其女儿送至急诊科。女儿报告说，在过去24小时内，该客户越来越困惑和嗜睡。女儿还注意到客户的尿量减少，饮食不佳。

content.en:
Vital Signs:
- Temperature: 38.9°C (102.0°F)
- Heart Rate: 118 beats/minute
- Respiratory Rate: 24 breaths/minute
- Blood Pressure: 94/52 mmHg
- SpO2: 91% on room air

Neurological:
- Alert to person only, disoriented to time and place. Restless.

Skin:
- Warm, flushed, and dry.

Urine:
- Dark amber, cloudy, with a strong odor.
content.zh:
生命体征：
- 体温：38.9°C (102.0°F)
- 心率：118次/分钟
- 呼吸频率：24次/分钟
- 血压：94/52 mmHg
- 血氧饱和度：室内空气下为91%

神经系统：
- 仅对人有反应，对时间和地点定向障碍。烦躁不安。

皮肤：
- 温暖、潮红、干燥。

尿液：
- 深琥珀色，浑浊，有浓烈气味。

content.en:
- White Blood Cell (WBC) Count: 18,500/mm³
- Lactate: 4.2 mmol/L
- Serum Creatinine: 1.8 mg/dL
- Blood Glucose: 210 mg/dL
- C-Reactive Protein (CRP): 150 mg/L
content.zh:
- 白细胞（WBC）计数：18,500/mm³
- 乳酸：4.2 mmol/L
- 血清肌酐：1.8 mg/dL
- 血糖：210 mg/dL
- C反应蛋白（CRP）：150 mg/L

content.en:
After a 500 mL bolus of 0.9% sodium chloride:
- Heart Rate: 124 beats/minute
- Respiratory Rate: 26 breaths/minute
- Blood Pressure: 88/48 mmHg
- Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP): 61 mmHg
content.zh:
给予500毫升0.9%氯化钠快速输注后：
- 心率：124次/分钟
- 呼吸频率：26次/分钟
- 血压：88/48 mmHg
- 平均动脉压（MAP）：61 mmHg

stem.en: Based on the initial triage assessment at 1400, for each finding, click to specify if it is consistent with classic SIRS criteria (a foundational infection-response framework still tested on the NCLEX-RN), organ dysfunction, or neither.
stem.zh: 根据1400时的初步分诊评估，对于每项发现，请点击指明其是否符合经典SIRS标准（一个在NCLEX-RN中仍会考核的基础性感染反应框架）、器官功能障碍或两者皆非。

rows.en: Temperature 38.9°C (102.0°F)
Heart Rate 118 beats/minute
rows.zh: 心率 118次/分钟

rows.en: Blood Pressure 94/52 mmHg
rows.zh: 血压 94/52 mmHg

rows.en: Altered mental status
rows.zh: 精神状态改变

rows.en: History of type 2 diabetes
rows.zh: 2型糖尿病史

correct.en: The client's fever, tachycardia, and tachypnea meet the criteria for SIRS. Hypotension and altered mental status are critical indicators of sepsis-induced organ dysfunction, signifying progression towards septic shock. The history of diabetes is a risk factor for infection but is not a direct finding of the current inflammatory response or organ failure. Note: The Sepsis-3 consensus (2016) defines sepsis by organ dysfunction rather than requiring SIRS criteria, using SOFA/qSOFA scoring. SIRS remains foundational for NCLEX-RN education and continues to appear in entry-level nursing curricula.
correct.zh: 客户的发烧、心动过速和呼吸急促符合SIRS的标准。低血压和精神状态改变是脓毒症引起的器官功能障碍的关键指标，标志着病情正向脓毒性休克发展。糖尿病史是感染的风险因素，但不是当前炎症反应或器官衰竭的直接发现。注意：Sepsis-3共识（2016年）以器官功能障碍（SOFA/qSOFA评分）而非SIRS标准定义脓毒症。SIRS仍是NCLEX-RN护理教育的基础内容，在初级护理课程中继续出现。

byChoice.en: A temperature above 38°C is a key criterion for SIRS, reflecting the body's systemic inflammatory response to infection.
byChoice.zh: 体温高于38°C是SIRS的一个关键标准，反映了身体对感染的全身性炎症反应。

byChoice.en: A heart rate above 90 beats/minute is another primary criterion for SIRS, indicating the cardiovascular system's compensatory response.
byChoice.zh: 心率超过90次/分钟是SIRS的另一个主要标准，表明心血管系统的代偿性反应。

byChoice.en: A systolic blood pressure below 100 mmHg is a sign of organ dysfunction, specifically cardiovascular dysfunction, indicating inadequate tissue perfusion.
byChoice.zh: 收缩压低于100 mmHg是器官功能障碍的标志，特别是心血管功能障碍，表明组织灌注不足。

byChoice.en: Altered mental status (confusion, lethargy) is a sign of central nervous system dysfunction due to hypoperfusion, a hallmark of severe sepsis.
byChoice.zh: 精神状态改变（困惑、嗜睡）是由于灌注不足导致的中枢神经系统功能障碍的标志，是严重脓毒症的特征。

byChoice.en: While diabetes is a significant risk factor that predisposes the client to infections and complicates management, it is part of the medical history, not a current sign of SIRS or organ dysfunction.
byChoice.zh: 虽然糖尿病是一个重要的风险因素，使客户易于感染并使管理复杂化，但它属于病史，而不是SIRS或器官功能障碍的当前体征。

testTakingStrategy.en: Differentiate between the definition of SIRS (systemic inflammation) and severe sepsis (inflammation plus organ dysfunction). SIRS criteria are specific vital sign and lab value thresholds. Organ dysfunction involves signs of failure in specific body systems (e.g., brain, kidneys, cardiovascular).
testTakingStrategy.zh: 区分SIRS（全身性炎症）和严重脓毒症（炎症加器官功能障碍）的定义。SIRS标准是特定的生命体征和实验室数值阈值。器官功能障碍涉及特定身体系统（如大脑、肾脏、心血管）衰竭的迹象。

glossary.termZh: 全身性炎症反应综合征
glossary.defZh: 机体对感染等损伤产生的过度全身性炎症反应的历史性框架。2016年Sepsis-3共识以器官功能障碍为核心重新定义脓毒症，但SIRS在护理基础教育中仍具参考价值。
由于疾病或损伤导致一个或多个器官功能异常，无法维持体内平衡。

stem.en: The provider has confirmed septic shock. The nurse should anticipate performing which actions in order of priority? Drag and drop the options into the correct sequence.
stem.zh: 医生已确认脓毒性休克。护士应按优先顺序预期执行哪些操作？将选项拖放到正确的顺序中。

options.en: Administer broad-spectrum antibiotics intravenously.
options.zh: 静脉注射广谱抗生素。

options.en: Initiate a rapid intravenous fluid bolus of 30 mL/kg.
options.zh: 开始快速静脉输注30 mL/kg的液体。

options.en: Prepare to administer a vasopressor infusion (e.g., norepinephrine).
options.zh: 准备给予血管加压药输注（如去甲肾上腺素）。

options.en: Obtain blood and urine cultures.
options.zh: 获取血培养和尿培养。

correct.en: The highest priority in septic shock is to restore tissue perfusion and intravascular volume, which is achieved by aggressive fluid resuscitation. Cultures must be obtained before starting antibiotics to identify the pathogen, but this should not delay treatment. Antibiotics should be administered within the first hour. Vasopressors are used after initial fluid resuscitation if hypotension persists, to maintain a target MAP of ≥ 65 mmHg.
correct.zh: 脓毒性休克的最高优先级是恢复组织灌注和血管内容量，这通过积极的液体复苏来实现。 必须在开始使用抗生素之前获取培养物以鉴定病原体，但这不应延迟治疗。抗生素应在第一个小时内给予。 如果低血压在初步液体复苏后持续存在，则使用血管加压药以维持≥ 65 mmHg的目标平均动脉压。

byChoice.en: Vasopressors are indicated for persistent hypotension (MAP < 65 mmHg) despite adequate fluid resuscitation. They are a secondary intervention after the initial fluid challenge has been completed or is underway.
byChoice.zh: 尽管进行了充分的液体复苏，但对于持续性低血压（MAP < 65 mmHg），应使用血管加压药。它们是在初步液体冲击完成或正在进行后的次要干预措施。

byChoice.en: Fluid resuscitation is the immediate priority to address the profound vasodilation and capillary leak, aiming to correct hypotension and improve organ perfusion.
byChoice.zh: 液体复苏是解决严重血管舒张和毛细血管渗漏的当务之急，旨在纠正低血压并改善器官灌注。

byChoice.en: Early administration of broad-spectrum antibiotics is crucial to control the source of infection and is a core measure in the sepsis bundle, ideally within one hour of recognition.
byChoice.zh: 早期给予广谱抗生素对于控制感染源至关重要，是脓毒症集束化治疗的核心措施，理想情况下应在识别后一小时内进行。

byChoice.en: Obtaining cultures is critical for guiding targeted antibiotic therapy. This should be done immediately after establishing IV access and before administering antibiotics, as long as it does not significantly delay antibiotic administration.
byChoice.zh: 获取培养物对于指导靶向抗生素治疗至关重要。这应在建立静脉通路后、给予抗生素前立即进行，只要不显著延迟抗生素的给予即可。

testTakingStrategy.en: For prioritization questions in shock states, follow the general principle of Circulation, Airway, Breathing, but apply it to the pathophysiology. In distributive shock like sepsis, restoring circulation (volume) is the most immediate life-saving action. Then, address the cause (infection) and use medications for refractory issues.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 对于休克状态下的优先排序问题，请遵循循环、气道、呼吸的一般原则，但要将其应用于病理生理学。在像脓毒症这样的分布性休克中，恢复循环（容量）是最直接的救生措施。然后，处理病因（感染）并使用药物治疗难治性问题。

glossary.defZh: 在休克等危急状态下，快速输注大量液体以恢复和维持循环血量和组织灌注。
一种能收缩血管、升高血压的药物，用于治疗液体复苏无效的休克。

stem.en: After two hours of aggressive fluid resuscitation and initiation of antibiotics and a norepinephrine infusion, which of the following findings would indicate clinical improvement? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 经过两小时的积极液体复苏、开始使用抗生素和去甲肾上腺素输注后，以下哪些发现表明临床状况有所改善？请选择所有适用项。

options.en: Lactate level has decreased to 2.0 mmol/L.
options.zh: 乳酸水平已降至2.0 mmol/L。

options.en: Skin is cool and clammy.
options.zh: 皮肤湿冷。

options.en: Urine output is 40 mL/hour.
options.zh: 尿量为40 mL/小时。

options.en: Serum creatinine remains 1.8 mg/dL.
options.zh: 血清肌酐仍为1.8 mg/dL。

options.en: Mean arterial pressure (MAP) is 70 mmHg.
options.zh: 平均动脉压（MAP）为70 mmHg。

options.en: Client is now oriented to person and place.
options.zh: 客户现在对人和地点有定向力。

correct.en: Positive outcomes in septic shock management are indicated by improved organ perfusion. This is evidenced by a MAP ≥ 65 mmHg, adequate urine output (≥ 0.5 mL/kg/hr or >30 mL/hr), improved mental status, and clearance of lactate. Cool, clammy skin suggests worsening shock (compensatory vasoconstriction). A stable but still elevated creatinine does not yet show improvement in renal function.
correct.zh: 脓毒性休克管理的积极结果表现为器官灌注的改善。这可以通过MAP ≥ 65 mmHg、足够的尿量（≥ 0.5 mL/kg/hr 或 >30 mL/hr）、改善的精神状态和乳酸清除来证明。湿冷的皮肤表明休克恶化（代偿性血管收缩）。稳定但仍升高的肌酐尚未显示肾功能改善。

byChoice.en: A decreasing lactate level indicates that anaerobic metabolism is resolving and tissue hypoxia is improving, which is a primary goal of therapy.
byChoice.zh: 乳酸水平下降表明无氧代谢正在消退，组织缺氧正在改善，这是治疗的主要目标。

byChoice.en: Improvement in mental status from confusion to increased orientation signifies better cerebral perfusion.
byChoice.zh: 精神状态从困惑改善为定向力增强，标志着脑灌注改善。

byChoice.en: Cool, clammy skin is a sign of compensatory or late-stage shock, where peripheral vasoconstriction shunts blood to the core. This would indicate worsening, not improvement.
byChoice.zh: 湿冷的皮肤是代偿性或晚期休克的标志，此时外周血管收缩将血液分流至核心。这表明病情恶化，而非改善。

byChoice.en: Urine output greater than 0.5 mL/kg/hr (approximately >30 mL/hr for most adults) is a key indicator of adequate renal perfusion.
byChoice.zh: 尿量大于0.5 mL/kg/hr（对大多数成年人约为 >30 mL/hr）是肾脏灌注充足的关键指标。

byChoice.en: An unchanged, elevated creatinine level indicates that the acute kidney injury has not yet resolved. An improvement would be a decrease in this value toward baseline.
byChoice.zh: 未改变的、升高的肌酐水平表明急性肾损伤尚未解决。改善的迹象是该值向基线下降。

byChoice.en: A MAP of ≥ 65 mmHg is the target for resuscitation in septic shock, indicating that blood pressure is adequate to perfuse vital organs.
byChoice.zh: MAP ≥ 65 mmHg是脓毒性休克复苏的目标，表明血压足以灌注重要器官。

testTakingStrategy.en: For 'evaluate outcomes' questions, think about the goals of the interventions. The goal of sepsis treatment is to restore perfusion. Ask yourself, 'Which of these findings shows that blood is getting to the organs (brain, kidneys, skin) and that cells are getting enough oxygen (lactate)?'
testTakingStrategy.zh: 对于“评估结果”类问题，请考虑干预措施的目标。脓毒症治疗的目标是恢复灌注。问问自己，“这些发现中，哪一个表明血液正在到达器官（大脑、肾脏、皮肤），并且细胞获得了足够的氧气（乳酸）？”

glossary.defZh: 一个心动周期内动脉血压的平均值，是衡量器官灌注压的重要指标。
组织缺氧时无氧代谢的产物，其水平升高是休克和组织灌注不足的标志。

stem.en: The client's MAP remains 60 mmHg despite receiving norepinephrine at 10 mcg/min. The provider orders the addition of a vasopressin infusion. What is the primary rationale for adding vasopressin in this clinical situation?
stem.zh: 尽管接受了10 mcg/min的去甲肾上腺素，客户的平均动脉压（MAP）仍为60 mmHg。医生下令加用血管加压素输注。在这种临床情况下，加用血管加压素的主要理由是什么？

options.en: To replace the body's depleted endogenous stores in a shock state.
options.zh: 补充休克状态下身体耗尽的内源性储备。

options.en: To allow for a lower dose of norepinephrine, reducing the risk of peripheral ischemia.
options.zh: 允许使用较低剂量的去甲肾上腺素，从而降低外周缺血的风险。

options.en: To directly stimulate beta-1 receptors and increase the heart rate.
options.zh: 直接刺激β-1受体，增加心率。

options.en: To provide a potent inotropic effect and increase cardiac contractility.
options.zh: 提供强效的正性肌力作用，增加心肌收缩力。

correct.en: In septic shock, there is profound vasodilation. Vasopressin is a non-catecholamine vasopressor that acts on V1 receptors, causing vasoconstriction through a different mechanism than norepinephrine (an alpha-agonist). Adding vasopressin can have a synergistic effect, raising blood pressure and allowing the dose of norepinephrine to be reduced. High doses of norepinephrine are associated with significant adverse effects, including severe peripheral vasoconstriction, tachyarrhythmias, and myocardial ischemia.
correct.zh: 在脓毒性休克中，存在严重的血管舒张。血管加压素是一种非儿茶酚胺类血管加压药，作用于V1受体，通过与去甲肾上腺素（一种α-激动剂）不同的机制引起血管收缩。加用血管加压素可以产生协同效应，升高血压，并允许减少去甲肾上腺素的剂量。高剂量的去甲肾上腺素与显著的不良反应相关，包括严重的外周血管收缩、快速性心律失常和心肌缺血。

byChoice.en: Vasopressin does not act on beta-1 receptors. Norepinephrine and epinephrine have beta-1 agonist effects that increase heart rate and contractility.
byChoice.zh: 血管加压素不作用于β-1受体。去甲肾上腺素和肾上腺素具有β-1激动剂作用，可增加心率和收缩力。

byChoice.en: While there is a state of relative vasopressin deficiency in septic shock, the primary therapeutic goal of administering it is its vasoconstrictive effect to raise MAP, not simply for replacement.
byChoice.zh: 虽然脓毒性休克中存在相对的血管加压素缺乏状态，但给予它的主要治疗目标是其血管收缩作用以提高MAP，而不仅仅是为了补充。

byChoice.en: This is the correct rationale. Adding a second vasopressor with a different mechanism of action can help achieve the target MAP while minimizing the toxicity of a single agent at a high dose. This is often referred to as a 'catecholamine-sparing' effect.
byChoice.zh: 这是正确的理由。添加具有不同作用机制的第二种血管加压药，有助于在实现目标MAP的同时，最大限度地减少单一药物高剂量时的毒性。这通常被称为“儿茶酚胺节约”效应。

byChoice.en: Vasopressin has minimal to no inotropic effects; its primary action is vasoconstriction. Inotropes like dobutamine would be used to increase contractility.
byChoice.zh: 血管加压素的正性肌力作用极小或没有；其主要作用是血管收缩。像多巴酚丁胺这样的正性肌力药物才用于增加收缩力。

testTakingStrategy.en: When multiple vasopressors are used, consider their different mechanisms of action. Norepinephrine is a primary alpha-agonist. Adding another agent like vasopressin suggests a need for a different pathway to achieve vasoconstriction, often to reduce the side effects of the primary agent.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当使用多种血管加压药时，请考虑它们不同的作用机制。去甲肾上腺素是主要的α-激动剂。添加像血管加压素这样的另一种药物，表明需要通过不同的途径来实现血管收缩，通常是为了减少主要药物的副作用。

glossary.defZh: 一种强效的α-肾上腺素能受体激动剂，主要引起外周血管收缩，是治疗脓毒性休克的首选血管加压药。
也称为抗利尿激素（ADH），在药理剂量下通过V1受体引起血管收缩，可作为脓毒性休克的二线血管加压药。

correct.en: This case illustrates the rapid identification and management of septic shock, emphasizing the progression from recognizing SIRS and organ dysfunction to implementing time-sensitive, prioritized interventions according to evidence-based guidelines, and finally, evaluating the response to complex pharmacotherapy.
correct.zh: 本案例展示了脓毒性休克的快速识别和管理，强调了从识别SIRS和器官功能障碍，到根据循证指南实施有时间敏感性的、优先的干预措施，最后评估对复杂药物治疗反应的整个过程。

testTakingStrategy.en: In unfolding case studies, track the client's data over time. Notice how the initial assessment findings (cues) lead to a hypothesis (sepsis), which is then confirmed by labs and worsening vitals (analysis), prompting a specific sequence of actions (intervention) whose effectiveness is then measured by specific endpoints (evaluation).
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在展开式案例研究中，要随时间追踪客户的数据。注意最初的评估发现（线索）如何导出一个假设（脓毒症），然后通过实验室检查和恶化的生命体征（分析）得到证实，从而促使采取一系列特定的行动（干预），其有效性再通过特定的终点（评估）来衡量。

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items.
stem.zh: 查看患者病历并回答病例分析题。

correct.en: This case study focuses on the recognition and nursing management of malignant spinal cord compression (MSCC) in an oncology patient. Prompt identification and emergent pharmacological and surgical interventions are critical to preventing permanent neurological loss.
correct.zh: 本病例分析侧重于肿瘤患者恶性脊髓压迫（MSCC）的识别和护理管理。及时识别并进行紧急药物和手术干预对于预防永久性神经功能丧失至关重要。

testTakingStrategy.en: Read the entire case sequentially. Note the trajectory of neurological findings, as decisions depend on recognizing the pattern of decline or improvement over time.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 按顺序阅读整个病例。注意神经系统发现的变化轨迹，因为决策取决于识别随时间推移的病情恶化或改善模式。

title.en: Spinal cord compression from metastatic cancer
title.zh: 转移性癌症引起的脊髓压迫

summary.en:
A 61-year-old woman with a three-year history of stage IV non-small cell lung cancer with known osseous metastases to the thoracic spine (T6, T8) and ribs is admitted to the oncology unit from the emergency department.
